column3: question
column4: choices
column6: explanation

column3: What are three necessary components for establishing a governance framework? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Automated Testing | B. Requirements Management | C. Change Control Log | D. Documentation Repository | E. Continuous Integration
column6: A governance framework requires Automated Testing (A) for quality assurance, a Change Control Log (C) to track approved changes, and a Documentation Repository (D) to record standards and policies. These three components ensure controlled, auditable, and repeatable governance across teams.

column3: Universal Containers' developers are working on a Visualforce page in a sandbox when an administrator adds a new field to Production. Which two approaches could an architect suggest to an administrator that would assist the developers in their development process? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use a Change Set to deploy the changes from Production to the sandbox, to ensure that changes made in production are reflected in the sandbox that the developers are working on | B. Use Salesforce-to-Salesforce to deploy the changes from Production to the sandbox, to ensure that changes made in production are reflected in the sandbox that the developers are working on | C. Manually replicate the same changes in the developer sandbox to ensure that changes made in production are reflected in the sandbox that the developers are working on | D. Refresh the developer sandbox to ensure that changes made in production are reflected in the sandbox that the developers are working on
column6: A Change Set can push production metadata down to a sandbox (A), ensuring sandbox developers have the latest production changes. Refreshing the developer sandbox (D) copies the current production metadata and data directly into the sandbox. Salesforce-to-Salesforce (B) is for sharing records between orgs, not deploying metadata.

column3: What is the responsibility of an executive sponsor on a project?
column4: A. Communicate project status | B. Determine project methodology | C. Design executive dashboards | D. Approve changes to project scope
column6: An executive sponsor's primary responsibility is approving changes to project scope (D). Communicating status (A) is the project manager's role; selecting methodology (B) is a PMO/team decision; designing dashboards (C) is an admin/developer task.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to shorten their deployment time to production by controlling which tests to run in production. UC's Architect has suggested that they run only subsets of tests. Which two statements are true regarding running specific tests during deployments? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. To run a subset of tests, set the Run Specified Tests test level on the DeployOptions objects and pass it as an argument to deploy() call. | B. run a subset of tests ,set the RunLocalTests test level on the DeployOptions object and pass it as an argument to deploy() call. | C. Specify both test classes and individual test methods that are required to be executed as both are supported in DeployOptions. | D. Specifying the test method is supported in DeployOptions, therefore specify only the test classes that are required to be executed.
column6: To run a subset of tests, you set the RunSpecifiedTests test level on DeployOptions and pass it to deploy() (A). DeployOptions supports specifying test classes but NOT individual test methods, so only test classes should be specified (D). Option C is incorrect because individual test methods are not supported.

column3: An Architect has been working on a large project for the past 6 months. This project must be live by the end of the current month. Which two planning techniques should the Architect use to ensure all metadata changes deploy smoothly and on time? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Ensure all code that is being deployed is checked into source control | B. Validate the final deployment package against production prior to go-live | C. Create a new sandbox and perform a test deployment to that environment | D. Upload a change set from sandbox to production as early as possible
column6: Checking all code into source control (A) ensures a complete, repeatable deployment package. Validating the final deployment package against production before go-live (B) surfaces issues early. Creating a new sandbox (C) and uploading change sets early (D) are not specifically effective planning techniques for smooth, on-time deployments.

column3: Universal Containers has automated its deployment process using Metadata API. However, they found that Metadata API doesn't support all the components yet. What should be done to address this?
column4: A. Deploy unsupported components manually before/after deployment. | B. Use AppExchange products to deploy unsupported components. | C. Use change sets for deploying all the unsupported components. | D. Use the force.com IDE for deploying the unsupported components.
column6: When Metadata API does not support certain components, the best practice is to deploy those components manually before or after the automated deployment and document the manual steps. AppExchange, change sets, and Force.com IDE do not solve the root coverage gap in Metadata API.

column3: Universal Containers has multiple minor and major releases in a year .Minor releases have sample configuration changes, while major releases involve large number of complex code components. What is the deployment tools should an architect recommend for both types of releases?
column4: A. Change sets for minor releases and Force.com IDE for major releases. | B. Change sets for both minor releases and major releases. | C. Change sets for minor releases and metadata API for major releases. | D. Force.com IDE for minor releases and metadata API for major releases.
column6: Change sets are simple and suitable for minor configuration releases. Metadata API (via Ant Migration Tool or Salesforce CLI) is the recommended tool for major releases with large numbers of complex components, enabling automation, scripting, and version control integration.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has integrated with their on-premise billing system using Salesforce Connect. The data is configured using an External Object in sandbox. UC wants to deploy the external object to production using the Metadata API and would like to know what Metadata types to choose for deployments to production. Which two options are valid metadata types related to deployment of external objects? Choose 2 answers.
column4: A. In change sets, external objects are included in the External Object component. | B. In the Metadata API, the Custom Object metadata type represents external objects. | C. In the Metadata API, the External Object metadata type represents external objects. | D. In change sets, external objects are included in the custom object component.
column6: In Metadata API, the CustomObject metadata type represents external objects (B), just as it does for custom objects. In change sets, external objects appear in the Custom Object component (D). There is no separate 'External Object' metadata type in Metadata API or change sets.

column3: Universal Containers is in the process of testing their integration between salesforce and their on-premise ERP systems. The testing team has requested a sandbox with up to 10,000 records in each object to benchmark the integration performance. What is the fastest approach an Architect should recommend?
column4: A. Spin off a partial copy sandbox using a sandbox template with all the objects required for testing the integration. | B. Spin off a Developer pro sandbox, migrate the metadata and load the data using data loader. | C. Spin off a Development sandbox, migrate the metadata and load the data using data loader. | D. Spin off a full copy sandbox with all the objects that are required for testing the integration.
column6: A Partial Copy sandbox with a sandbox template containing the relevant objects provides up to 10,000 records per object and provisions much faster than a Full sandbox. Developer Pro or Developer sandboxes (B,C) require manual data loading with Data Loader which is slower. A Full sandbox (D) takes significantly longer to provision and refresh.

column3: Universal Containers has started building a customer Lightning community that contains a few dozen Aura components. The development team lead has come to the Salesforce architect about questions regarding testing the Lightning components.What two knowledge points can the architect pass to the development team lead?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. There is a $T test helper object that can be used to create the instance of the Lightning component, and it is promise enabled. | B. The testing can be viewed in the lightning.force.com/c/jasminetests.app URL. The page loads and runs Jasmine test and writes pass/fail information to the screen. | C. The testing of the JavaScript part of the Aura component can be tested in the Jest framework and the Apex controllers can be tested using test classes. | D. Install the Lightning test service AppExchange package to enable the Aura component testing.
column6: The $T (now $A) test helper in Lightning Testing Service creates component instances and is promise-enabled (A). Aura component JavaScript can be tested with Jest via the Lightning Testing Service, and Apex controllers are tested with Apex test classes (C). Option D (installing the LTS AppExchange package) is the setup step, not a knowledge point about testing capabilities.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is implementing Salesforce and wants the custom code to be unit tested for all the adverse conditions. Which two best practices should an Architect recommend while implementing Test Classes? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Test classes should not create custom setting data. | B. Test classes must use existing data in the environment. | C. Execute test classes under various profiles | D. Test data must have positive as well as negative data.
column6: Executing test classes under various profiles (C) ensures that permission-based scenarios are covered. Test data must include both positive and negative cases (D) to validate expected outcomes and error handling. Custom setting data CAN be created in test classes (A is incorrect). Using existing org data (B) violates test isolation best practices.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has received feedback from the field on several new feature requests that in business goals, UC is looking for a way to quickly get feedback and prioritize these requests. Which two options should an Architect recommend? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Present the feature requests at a Center of Excellence meeting. | B. Create design standards around the new features being requested. | C. Send the requests to IT for a formal review at the end of the year. | D. Bring the feature request to the Test Manager to gain quality checks. | E. Create the backlog or priority list in a project management tool.
column6: Presenting feature requests at a Center of Excellence meeting (A) engages the governance body for proper evaluation and prioritization. Creating a backlog or priority list in a project management tool (E) formalizes tracking and enables stakeholder alignment. Taking requests to the Test Manager (D) is incorrect — the Test Manager role is not responsible for feature prioritization. [CORRECTED from A,D to A,E]

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is embarking on a large program of work, with different projects and different vendors. UC created a center of excellence (COE) that is struggling with scope creep between the different projects.What role should the architect suggest be added to the COE?
column4: A. Change managers | B. Scrum master | C. Release managers | D. Product owner
column6: A Change Manager (A) is the appropriate role to add to a COE struggling with scope creep between projects — they oversee the change control process, evaluate impact of scope changes, and prevent unauthorized scope expansion across multiple projects. A Scrum Master (B) operates within a single scrum team. Release Managers (C) focus on deployments. Product Owners (D) own the backlog for one product stream. [CORRECTED from B to A]

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is considering updating their Salesforce Release Management process. Which three best practices should UC consider for Version Control? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Maintain a single repository for application with individual branches for projects. | B. Maintain separate developer branches for minor and major releases. | C. Maintain unrestricted access to the release sandboxes for all changes being deployed. | D. Maintain a single entry point for production from the master branch. | E. Automation is a must with various application branches in the repository.
column6: Maintaining a single repository with individual branches per project (A) enables isolation. Maintaining separate developer branches for minor/major releases (B) supports controlled merging. A single entry point to production from the master branch (D) enforces a controlled release gate. Unrestricted access to release sandboxes (C) introduces significant deployment risk.

column3: Universal containers is looking to install a new application to enable advanced quoting in its current Professional Edition org. The org is near capacity with object and tab limits. Which two solutions should the Architect recommend? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create and install an unmanaged package | B. Upgrade to an Enterprise Edition org | C. Install an Aloha certified App | D. Buy more user licenses to increase org limits
column6: Upgrading to Enterprise Edition (B) removes Professional Edition object and tab limits. Installing an Aloha Certified AppExchange app (C) is valid because certified AppExchange managed packages do not count against Professional Edition custom object/tab limits. Simply buying user licenses (D) does not increase object or tab limits. An unmanaged package (A) would consume more of the limited slots.

column3: Universal Containers is a global organization that maintains regional production instances of Salesforce. One region has created a new application to track shipping containers.The CIO has requested that this new application be used globally by all the Salesforce instances and further maintained and modified regionally by local administrators.Which two deployment tools will support the request?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Change Sets B | B. ANT Migration Tool | C. VS Code with Salesforce Extension | D. Developer Console
column6: ANT Migration Tool (B) is scriptable and can deploy metadata to any Salesforce org globally. VS Code with Salesforce Extension (C) also supports retrieving and deploying metadata to any authorized org via SFDX CLI. Change sets (A) are restricted to orgs within the same Salesforce org network (same instance). Developer Console (D) cannot cross-org deploy.

column3: Universal Containers has several concurrent projects building new functionality, fixing bugs, and modifying existing functionality. Management would like features to be available to users as quickly as possible, even if the entire project is incomplete. What should an Architect recommend to maintain quality?
column4: A. Require developers to deploy completed code and unit tests directly to production | B. Use a spreadsheet to track approved changes that should be released with change sets | C. Deploy all functionality together to ensure all functionality works together without error | D. Utilize automated source control, test, and build systems to test and deploy to production
column6: Automated source control, test, and build systems (CI/CD pipelines) enable features to be deployed independently as soon as they are complete, while maintaining quality through automated testing. This satisfies both the speed and quality requirements. Manual spreadsheets (B) are error-prone, deploying directly to production (A) skips QA, and waiting for all functionality (C) defeats the goal of rapid availability.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has used Salesforce for the last 6 years with 50% custom code. UC has recently implemented continuous integration. UC wants to improve old test classes whenever new functionality invalidates tests. UC also wants to reduce the deployment time required. What should Architect recommend?
column4: A. Test classes cannot be executed in sandboxes. | B. A Do not execute any test classes in sandboxes and Production. | C. Do not execute test classes in sandboxes and all test classes in Production. | D. Execute all test classes in sandboxes and selected test classes in Production.
column6: Running all test classes in sandboxes validates full coverage and catches regressions during development. Running selected (specified) tests in production reduces deployment time while satisfying Salesforce's 75% coverage requirement. Tests CAN be run in sandboxes (A,B are wrong). Running no tests in production (C) is invalid; all tests would be required if RunSpecifiedTests is not used.

column3: Universal Containers recently added a new sales division to theEnsure that Record Type Ids match both Productiowhen migrating to Production, the Developer reports that Unit Tests are failing. What should an Architect do to ensure tests execute predictably?
column4: A. Ensure executed Apex test run as valid users | B. Ensure unit tests execute with seeAllData=true | C. Ensure that Record Type Ids match both Production and Sandbox orgs | D. Ensure unit tests generate their own test data
column6: Unit tests should generate their own test data (D) to ensure predictability regardless of org state, including Record Type IDs that can differ between environments. Using seeAllData=true (B) causes test failures if expected data is absent. Manually matching Record Type IDs (C) is fragile and does not scale across environments.

column3: At any given time, Universal Containers has 10 Apex developers building new functionality and fixing bugs. Which branching strategy should an Architect recommend that mitigates the risk of developers overwriting others changes?
column4: A. Have all developers work in the same branch, continuously testing for regressions | B. Have developers work in separate branches and merge their changes in a common branch for testing | C. Have all developers build new functionality in new branches, but fix bugs in the HEAD | D. Don't use source control. Rely on Salesforce's built-in conflict detection mechanism
column6: Having developers work in separate branches and merge into a common integration branch for testing (B) is the standard branching strategy that prevents developers from overwriting each other's changes. Working in the same branch (A) leads to conflicts. Relying on Salesforce's conflict detection (D) is not a version control strategy. Option C creates inconsistency between feature and bug fix branches.

column3: Salesforce has three major releases a year.Which type of change introduced by a release can cause automated browser tests to need updating?
column4: A. Metadata schema changes | B. New standard fields | C. DOM changes | D. New Apex methods
column6: DOM (Document Object Model) changes in Salesforce releases alter the HTML structure, element IDs, and CSS selectors used by the UI, which breaks automated browser tests that rely on those selectors. Metadata schema changes (A) affect API tools. New fields (B) and Apex methods (D) do not impact browser-level UI test scripts.

column3: A developer was trying to retrieve the metadata from an org and ran the sfdx force:source:retrieve command. When the command was run, the developer received the error message: "This command is required to run from within an SFDX project" What can be two possible reasons that caused this problem? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The developer forgot to add the -n option with a project name as a command line argument. | B. The developer created the project within VSCode, but ran the command in a separate terminal. | C. The developer created the project, but ran the command outside of the project directory. | D. The developer hadn't run the sfdx force:project:create command.
column6: The error 'required to run from within an SFDX project' occurs when the developer ran the command outside the project directory (C) or never created the project using sfdx force:project:create (D). Running in a separate terminal (B) only causes the issue if the working directory is wrong. The -n option (A) is for project creation, not source retrieval.

column3: Universal Containers has automated its deployment process using Metadata API. However, they found that Metadata API doesn't support all the components yet. What should be done to address this?
column4: A. Deploy unsupported components manually before/after deployment. | B. Use AppExchange products to deploy unsupported components. | C. Use the force.com IDE for deploying the unsupported components. | D. Use change sets for deploying all the unsupported components.
column6: Same principle as Q6: when Metadata API does not support certain components, the recommended approach is to deploy those components manually before or after the API-driven deployment. This is the documented Salesforce best practice for unsupported metadata types.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is preparing for the new Salesforce release in a couple of months, and has several ongoing development projects that may be affected. Which three steps should the team at UC take to prepare for this release? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Upgrade any SOAP integrations to the newest WSDL as early as possible | B. Run regression tests in an upgraded sandbox to detect any issues with the Upgrade. | C. Refresh a Sandbox during the Release Preview Window to ensure they have the upcoming release. | D. Review the release notes for automatically-enabled features and technical debt. | E. Contact Salesforce to schedule a time to upgrade the full Sandbox.
column6: Running regression tests in an upgraded sandbox (B) detects issues with the upcoming release. Refreshing a sandbox during the Preview Window (C) gives access to the pre-release version for early testing. Reviewing release notes for auto-enabled features and technical debt (D) is essential preparation. Upgrading SOAP WSDLs too early (A) is risky; scheduling a pod upgrade (E) is not a customer option.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is implementing Salesforce for the first time. Their legacy CRM system is an on premise home-grown application written in Java. UC plans to implement a continuous integration process that mirrors their current standard. Under what conditions should an Architect recommend against continuous integration?
column4: A. The client does not have budget for additional software | B. Test scripts will be generated as part of the testing phase | C. There isn't a full sandbox available to leverage | D. The Salesforce instance has only standard functionality
column6: CI provides the most value when there is custom code to compile, test, and automate. For an org with only standard Salesforce functionality (D), CI adds process overhead without proportional benefit since there is no custom code to test. Budget constraints (A), test script availability (B), and lack of a full sandbox (C) are not valid contraindications for CI when custom code exists.

column3: Universal Containers has dozens of independent user acceptance and functional teams that need to test independently in isolation, and on current production data that was modified within the last week. Which Sandbox type should a Technical Architect recommend?
column4: A. Developer Pro Sandbox | B. Partial Copy Sandbox | C. Developer Sandbox | D. Full Sandbox
column6: A Full sandbox is required when dozens of independent teams need both isolation AND current production data updated within the last week. Only a Full sandbox provides a complete copy of production data that can be refreshed regularly. Partial Copy sandboxes (B) have data volume limits. Developer/Developer Pro sandboxes (A,C) have very limited data storage capacity.

column3: Universal Containers has written several validation rules and workflow rules for the lead object. Which two test types should an Architect suggest to ensure that a large inbound call center does not experience platform slowdowns under high call volume for the Lead object? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Unit Test | B. Performance Test | C. Load Test | D. Stress Test
column6: Performance testing (B) measures response time and throughput under realistic load, directly relevant to response time SLAs. Load testing (C) measures system behavior under the expected peak concurrent user volume. Unit tests (A) test logic in isolation. Stress testing (D) pushes beyond normal operating limits and is a separate concern from measuring response time under expected load.

column3: Universal Containers has a highly customized Salesforce org, with many different pieces of configuration and code. Which configuration item should be covered by executable tests?
column4: A. Active Process Builders | B. Validation Rules | C. Workflow Rules | D. Case Assignment Rules
column6: Active Process Builders (and Flows) contain executable logic that can and should be covered by automated test execution. Validation rules (B), workflow rules (C), and assignment rules (D) are declarative configurations that execute through DML operations in test classes but are not themselves executable test targets — they are tested indirectly.

column3: At Universal Containers, Salesforce administrators are making changes to the permission sets under instruction from the business. Randomly, various SOQL statements are failing. What strategy could be advised to bring this issue to the developer's attention earlier?
column4: A. Ask administrators to only make changes to profiles instead. | B. Create a sandbox refresh strategy to ensure each sandbox is refreshed every day. | C. Extract each permission set, commit and merge to source control, and run through CI checks. | D. Advice developers to switch to SOSL queries that are more robust instead.
column6: Extracting permission sets, committing them to source control, and running them through CI checks (C) creates an automated pipeline that catches permission set changes breaking SOQL queries early, before they reach production. Switching to profiles (A) doesn't solve the root issue. Daily sandbox refreshes (B) are excessive overhead. Switching to SOSL (D) avoids fixing the underlying problem.

column3: When replacing an old legacy system with Salesforce, which two strategies should the plan consider to mitigate the risks associated with migrating data from the legacy system to Salesforce? Choose two answers
column4: A. Migrate users in phases based on their functions, requiring parallel use of legacy system and Salesforce for certain period of time. | B. Identify the data relevant to the new system, including dependencies, and develop a plan/scripts for verification of data integrity. | C. Use a full sandbox environment for all the systems involved, a full deployment plan with test data generation scripts, and full testing including integrations. | D. Use a full sandbox environment and perform test runs of data migration scripts/processes with real data from the legacy system.
column6: Migrating users in phases with parallel operation of legacy and Salesforce systems (A) reduces cutover risk. Performing test runs of migration scripts with real production data in a full sandbox (D) validates scripts and data quality before go-live. Option B (identifying data relevance) is important planning but less specifically about mitigating migration risk. Option C describes a testing environment strategy rather than a data migration risk mitigation.

column3: In the effort of improving the code quality, Universal Containers (UC) has asked a third-party system integrator to perform some independent code reviews. One piece of the feedback is the development team is seemingly not doing enough negative unit testing. Which are three usual symptoms of inadequate negative tests?
column4: A. Developers often have to turn to the debug log for details of the failed Apex executions. | B. When an Apex batch job runs at a scheduled time, an increased number of Apex execution errors occur over all. | C. An Apex process runs into an un-handled exception when an HTTP callout has an unexpected status code in the response body. | D. Developers constantly ask the testers for a screenshot of the error and the exact steps of reproducing the error. | E. The delivered user interfaces are regularly not meeting the expectations of the business users.
column6: Symptoms of inadequate negative testing include: relying on debug logs for failed execution details (A) indicating missing exception handling tests; increased batch job errors (B) indicating untested edge cases in async processing; and unhandled exceptions for unexpected HTTP status codes (C) indicating missing negative callout test coverage. Options D and E relate to communication and UI expectations, not negative unit test coverage gaps.

column3: The team at Universal Containers is building an application on Java that will interact with its Salesforce application. They want to use SOQL queries to retrieve and make changes to smaller pieces of Salesforce metadata through this application. Which API should the team leverage?
column4: A. Tooling API | B. User Interface API | C. Metadata API | D. Any Salesforce API
column6: The Tooling API is purpose-built for querying and manipulating Salesforce metadata via SOQL-like queries (e.g., querying ApexClass, CustomField records), making it ideal for client applications that need to retrieve and modify smaller pieces of metadata. The Metadata API (C) uses XML/ZIP-based retrieve/deploy operations rather than SOQL.

column3: By to What three tools should an architect recommend to support application lifecycle methodology?
column4: A. Database management systems | B. Version control repository | C. Middleware | D. Continuous integration tool | E. Issue tracking tool
column6: A Version Control Repository (B) tracks all code and configuration changes with history. A Continuous Integration tool (D) automates builds, tests, and validation. An Issue Tracking tool (E) manages work items, defects, and features. Database management systems (A) and middleware (C) are infrastructure components, not ALM tooling.

column3: Universal Containers is about to begin Development work on a new project in their Salesforce org that will take many months to complete. UC is concerned about how critical bugs will be addressed for existing live functionality. What is the recommended release management strategy to address this concern?
column4: A. Include fixes for critical bugs in the ongoing Development sandboxes so that they will be released with the other code. | B. Utilize a dedicated developer pro sandbox to address critical bugs and release to production. | C. Address critical bugs in the Development sandboxes and push those changes to production separately. | D. Keep teams separate until the end of the project and create a Full Copy sandbox to merge their work then.
column6: Using a dedicated Developer Pro sandbox for critical hotfixes (B) allows urgent production bugs to be fixed and tested in isolation, then deployed quickly without being blocked by or disrupting ongoing development work in other sandboxes. This is the standard hotfix branch/environment strategy.

column3: Metadata API supports deploy () and retrieve () calls for file-based deployment. Which two scenarios are the primary use cases for writing code to call retrieve () and deploy () methods directly? Choose two answers
column4: A. Team development of an application in a Developer Edition organization. After completing development and testing, the application is Distributed via Lightning Platform AppExchange. | B. Development of a custom application in a scratch org. After completing development and testing, the application is then deployed into an upper sandbox using Salesforce CLI (SFDX) | C. Development of a customization in a sandbox organization. The deployment team then utilize the Ant Migration Tool to deploy the customization to an upper sandbox for testing. | D. Development of a custom application in a sandbox organization. After completing development and testing, the application is then deployed Into a production organization using Metadata API.
column6: Writing code to call retrieve() and deploy() directly is appropriate for: using the Ant Migration Tool which wraps these API calls (C); or building a custom application that deploys to a production org (D). AppExchange package distribution (A) uses the packaging framework, not direct API calls. SFDX scratch org deployments (B) use the CLI which abstracts the API calls differently.

column3: What two things are required to delete metadata using a deploy() call in Salesforce? Choose two answers.
column4: A. Package.XML file. | B. The CURRENT API version must be used. | C. DestructiveChanges.xml file. | D. PurgeOnDelete option must be set to TRUE.
column6: To delete metadata via deploy(), a package.xml manifest (A) is required to define the deployment structure, and a destructiveChanges.xml file (C) lists the components to be deleted. The PurgeOnDelete option (D) is optional (determines whether deleted components bypass the recycle bin). Using the current API version (B) is not a strict requirement.

column3: Which two ways should a developer working on a data loading integration that operates between different Salesforce environments insert multiple related records in one call or transaction?
column4: A. REST API SObject Tree Request | B. Bulk API 2.0 | C. REST API Composite Request | D. Streaming API
column6: REST API SObject Tree Request (A) inserts a parent record with related children in a single API call, preserving relationships. REST API Composite Request (C) bundles multiple API operations into a single HTTP request that can be executed transactionally. Bulk API 2.0 (B) is for large-volume asynchronous operations. Streaming API (D) is for event-driven notifications, not record inserts.

column3: Since Universal Containers (UC) has adopted agile methodologies, the CEO is requesting the development teams to deliver more and more work in shorter time frames. The CTO responds by saying the developers are not able to deliver the jobs they are committing to. What evidence can be gathered in an agile tool to support the CTO’s claims?
column4: A. The definition of done (DoD) | B. A burndown chart showing team finishes early sprint after sprint | C. A Kanban board showing there’s always the maximum allowed amount of work in progress (WIP) | D. column6: A burndown chart showing the team consistently missing their sprint forecast sprint after sprint (D) provides empirical, quantitative evidence that developers are not delivering what they commit to — directly supporting the CTO's position. A Kanban WIP board (C) shows flow issues but is less specific to sprint commitment. Option A and B do not directly address delivery performance.

column3: Universal Containers has a highly customized Salesforce org, with many different pieces of configuration and code.Which configuration item should be covered by executable tests?
column4: A. Active Process Builders | B. Validation Rules | C. Workflow Rules | D. Case Assignment Rules Expose Correct Answer Answer : A Next Question
column6: Active Process Builders contain executable automation logic that should be covered by executable tests. Validation rules, workflow rules, and case assignment rules are declarative configurations tested indirectly through DML in test classes, not directly by executable test frameworks.

column3: The CTO at Universal Containers decided to implement the Scrum framework for its agile teams, and communicated a set of Scrum principles to the company.Which describes a Scrum principle?
column4: A. Respect other teams by not doing their work (a developer should not test the software). | B. Create transparency by being honest and clear about timing, planning, and obstacles. | C. Embrace change by working on a different scope every day. | D. Deliver working software, so if a software component is working, avoid
column6: A Scrum principle is creating transparency by being honest and clear about timing, planning, and obstacles (B). Scrum encourages cross-functional teams where developers also test (A is anti-Scrum). Working on different scope daily (C) violates the sprint commitment principle. Avoiding changes to working software (D) contradicts the agile principle of welcoming change.

column3: Universal Containers has discovered a Sev0 defect in production. Tens of thousands of records will be created with incorrect data in minutes, producing significant brand damage as a consequence. The Salesforce administrator has suggested that the defective text field be replaced with a new picklist field directly in production. The page layout will be modified so that the text-field is removed and the new picklist field added.What should the Salesforce architect advise?
column4: A. Explain that only deve
column6: Even for a Sev0 defect, the architect should advise against making direct production changes without going through a proper hotfix process (D). Proper practice is to fix and test in a hotfix sandbox, then deploy to production. Making ad hoc field type changes directly in production can cause additional data integrity issues and violates governance.

column3: Which two options should be considered when making production changes in a highly regulated and audited environment? (Choose two.)
column4: A. All changes including hotfixes should be reviewed against security principles. | B. After deployment, the development team should test and verity functionality in production. | C. Any production change should have explicit stakeholder approval. | D. No manual steps should be carried out. Expose Correct Answer Answer : AC Next Question
column6: In a highly regulated environment, all production changes including hotfixes must be reviewed against security principles (A) to prevent introducing vulnerabilities. Any production change requires explicit stakeholder approval (C) for auditability and compliance. Testing in production (B) is poor practice. Prohibiting all manual steps (D) is impractical for every scenario.

column3: Which two actions will contribute to an improvement of code security? (Choose two.)
column4: A. Use two developers to review and fix current code vulnerabilities. | B. Implement a pull request and secure code review. | C. Hire a company specialized in secure code review the current code. | D. Integrate a static code security analysis tool in the CI/CD process. Expose Correct Answer Answer : BD Next Question
column6: A pull request with secure code review (B) provides human oversight before code is merged, catching security issues that automated tools may miss. Integrating a static code security analysis tool in CI/CD (D) automatically and consistently detects common security vulnerabilities. Having two developers review and fix (A) is reactive not systemic. Hiring an external company (C) is a one-time audit, not an ongoing process improvement.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has multiple development teams that work on separate streams of work, with different timelines. Each stream has different releases of code and config, and the delivery dates differ between them.What is a suitable branching policy to recommend?
column4: A. GitHub flow | B. Trunk-based development | C. Scratch-org-based development | D. Leaf-based development Expose Correct Answer Answer : A Next Question
column6: GitHub flow is a lightweight, feature-branch-based strategy where each stream of work has its own branch and merges to main when ready, making it suitable for multiple concurrent streams with different timelines. Trunk-based development (B) minimizes branching. Scratch org development (C) is an environment model. Leaf-based development (D) is not a recognized branching strategy.

column3: Cloud Kicks is switching to Salesforce from a different CRM. They have existing datasets for all standard Salesforce objects.In which optimized order should the architect recommend these objects be loaded?
column4: A. Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Products, Opportunities, Opportunity Line Items | B. Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities, Products, Opportunity Line Items, Leads | C. Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, Products, Opportunity Line Items | D. Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Products, Opportunities, Oppor
column6: The correct data loading order respects foreign key dependencies: Accounts first (no dependencies), then Contacts (need Account IDs), then Opportunities (need Account IDs), then Products, then Opportunity Line Items (need both Opportunity and Product IDs), with Leads last (independent objects). Answer B follows this dependency order.

column3: Universal Containers wants to introduce data volume testing to resolve ongoing performance defects earlier in the lifecycle. Regulations prohibit the use of production data in non-production environments.Which two options can the architect recommend? (Choose two.)
column4: A. Request a partial Sandbox copy after the next Salesforce release. | B. Generate mock data that mimics production data shape and volume. | C. Use Query Analyzer in production. | D. Perform data masking on full sandbox after a refresh. Expo
column6: Generating mock data that mimics production data shape and volume (B) complies with data privacy regulations while enabling realistic volume testing. Performing data masking on a full sandbox after refresh (D) anonymizes sensitive production data while preserving realistic data volume and relationships. A partial sandbox (A) has record count limits. Using Query Analyzer in production (C) would test against live production data.

column3: What are three advantages of using the SFDX? (Choose three.)
column4: A. Can quickly deploy metadata using Execute Anonymous. | B. Can create scratch orgs. | C. Can install application metadata from a central repository. | D. Can store code on a local machine, or a version control system. | E. Can use native Deployment Rollback Too to quickly revert to prior state. Expose Correct Answer Answer : BCD Next Question
column6: SFDX advantages include: creating scratch orgs (B) for isolated, version-controlled development environments; installing application metadata from a central repository (C) via package installation; storing code locally or in a VCS (D). Execute Anonymous (A) is an Apex debugging feature, not an SFDX advantage. Native deployment rollback (E) is not a standard SFDX feature.

column3: There are many types of quality assurance techniques that can help minimize defects in software projects.Which two techniques should an architect recommend, for Universal Containers to incorporate into its overall CI/CD pipeline? (Choose two.)
column4: A. Automated browser testing | B. Stress testing | C. Business verification testing | D. Static code quality analysis Expose Correct Answer Answer : AD Next Question
column6: Automated browser testing (A) validates UI functionality and catches regressions from DOM changes across releases. Static code quality analysis (D) catches code defects and security issues early in the pipeline. Stress testing (B) requires special infrastructure and permissions. Business verification testing (C) is typically manual UAT, not CI/CD pipeline testing.

column3: Universal Containers’s architect is documenting the application lifecycle management (ALM) process to communicate it to the development teams from different implementation partners.Which three steps apply to any Salesforce development project? (Choose three.)
column4: A. Test | B. Develop | C. Continuous Integration | D. Build Release | E. Change Sets Expose Correct Answer Answer : ABD Next Question
column6: The three universal Salesforce ALM steps that apply to any project are: Test (A), Develop (B), and Build Release (D). Continuous Integration (C) is a methodology/practice that supports the ALM process. Change Sets (E) is a deployment tool, not an ALM step. The Salesforce ALM framework explicitly identifies Develop, Test, and Build Release as the core repeatable steps.

column3: A team has completed a sprint and intends to deploy these changes after business approval, but they will immediately begin the next sprint.What strategy should an architect recommend?
column4: A. Using Git, create a release branch from the develop branch. All fixes must be made in the release branch. After deployment, merge release with develop. | B. The first task of the new sprint must be the deployment approval. After that, the other tasks of the sprint can be performed in the environments and Git. | C. C
column6: Creating a release branch from the develop branch (A) is the Git Flow approach — it isolates release-ready code, allows bug fixes in the release branch without contaminating ongoing sprint work, and merges back into develop after production deployment. This is the standard release management branching pattern.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) have developed a managed package targeted for AppExchange. The product includes some Apex code to customize and create layouts. UC is in the testing phase of the package, so it’s not certified yet. During testing on the target org, the Apex code for the layouts fails.Why are the Apex classes not able to access the metadata of the target org during testing?
column4: A. Apex Settings to allow the access to metadata is not switched on. | B. UC needs to get the managed package certifi
column6: Apex code in uncertified managed beta packages cannot access the metadata of the target org. The package must be certified/approved by Salesforce before its Apex can access target org metadata through the Metadata API. This is a security restriction on uncertified packages.

column3: A developer was trying to retrieve the metadata from an org and ran the sfdx force:source:retrieve command. When the command was run, the developer received the error message: “This command is required to run from within an SFDX project.”What can be two possible reasons that caused this problem? (Choose two.)
column4: A. The developer hadn’t run the sfdx force:project:create command. | B. The developer created the project within VSCode, but ran the command in a separate terminal. | C. The developer created t
column6: The SFDX error 'required to run from within an SFDX project' occurs because: the developer never created a project with sfdx force:project:create (A), so no sfdx-project.json exists; or the developer ran the command from outside the project directory (C). Running in a separate terminal (B) only matters if the directory is wrong. The -n flag is for project creation, not retrieval.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) recently acquired Eastern Trail Outfitters (ETO). NTO's sales leadership team had hands-on experience with the ETO's Sales Optimization app and have given the feedback that the app would benefit NTO's sales team. Which option should the architect recommend for having ETO's Sales Optimization app in NTO's Salesforce org in the shortest possible time?
column4: A. Create users in ETO's org and provide access to NTO's sales team. | B. Create a core team and build the Sales Optimization app in NTO's org. | C. Create a managed package of the app and deploy in NTO's org. | D. Create an unmanaged package of the app and deploy in NTO's org.
column6: An unmanaged package is the fastest way to package and deploy an existing Salesforce app to another org without going through AppExchange certification. It can be created and installed quickly, and all components remain fully editable in the target org. Managed packages (C) require a longer setup and certification process.

column3: Which are two recommended methods of creating test data in Salesforce? (Choose two.)
column4: A. Host a mock endpoint to produce sample information from an endpoint. | B. Reference data from middleware directly within your test class. | C. Utilize Heroku Connect to provide test class data. | D. Load a CSV as a static resource and reference it in a test class.
column6: The two recommended Salesforce methods for creating test data are: referencing data from a test data factory class (B) — a reusable class with @isTest that creates standard test records programmatically; and loading a CSV file as a static resource referenced in test classes (D) using Test.loadData(). Mock endpoints (A) are for HTTP callout mocking. Heroku Connect (C) is a data sync product.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has recently acquired other companies that have their own Salesforce orgs. These companies have been merged as new UC business units. The CEO has requested an architect to review the org strategy. taking into consideration two main factors: The CEO wants business process standardization among all business units. Business process integration is not required as the different business units have different customers and expertise. Which org strategy should the architect recommend in this scenario, and why?
column4: A. Single-org strategy, as costs increase as the number of orgs go up. | B. Multi-org strategy, as it is uncommon for the diversified business units to get used to working in the same space as the other business units. | C. Multi-org strategy, as they could deploy a common managed package into the orgs of the different business units. | D. Single-org strategy, as the high level of business process standardization will be easier to implement in a single org.
column6: A single-org strategy is recommended when there is a high level of required business process standardization (D), because a single org enforces consistent processes and a unified data model across all business units. Multi-org strategies add complexity without justification when standardization is the primary goal.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is planning to move to Salesforce Sales Cloud and retire its homegrown on-premise system. As part of the project, UC will need to migrate 5 million Accounts, 10 million Contacts, and 5 million Leads to Salesforce. Which three areas should be tested as part of data migration? (Choose three.)
column4: A. Account and Lead ownership | B. Contact association with correct Account | C. Page layout assignments | D. Lead assignment | E. Data transformation against source system
column6: Key areas to test in a data migration: Contact association with the correct Account (B) verifies relational data integrity; Lead assignment (D) validates that assignment rules work correctly post-migration; Data transformation against the source system (E) confirms field mappings and data conversions are accurate. Page layout assignments (C) are configuration concerns. Account/Lead ownership (A) is partially covered by D.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a large user base (>300 users) and was originally implemented eight years ago by a Salesforce Systems Integration Partner. Since then, UC has made a number of changes to their Visual force pages and Apex classes in response to customer requirements, made by a variety of Vendors and internal teams. Which three issues would a new Technical Architect expect to see when evaluating the code in the Salesforce org? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Multiple triggers on the same object, making it hard to understand the order of operations. | B. Multiple unit test failures would be encountered. | C. Broken functionality due to Salesforce upgrades. | D. Duplicated logic across Visual force pages and Apex classes performing similar tasks. | E. Custom built JSON and String manipulation Classes that are no longer required.
column6: In a long-running, multi-vendor org, common code quality issues include: multiple triggers on the same object (A) creating unpredictable execution order; duplicated logic across Visualforce pages and Apex (D) indicating lack of shared utilities or services; custom-built utility classes now superseded by platform capabilities (E) representing accumulated technical debt. Unit test failures (B) would prevent deployment. Broken functionality (C) may or may not exist.

column3: Universal Containers uses multiple Salesforce orgs for its different lines of business (LOBs). In a recent analysis, the architect found that UC could have a more complete view of its customers by gathering customer data from different orgs. What two options can an architect recommend to accomplish the customer 360-degree view? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Implement a Complete Graph multi-org strategy by allowing each org to connect directly to every other, reading and writing customer data from the orgs where it has been created | B. Implement a Hub-and-Spoke multi-org strategy by replicating customer data from each LOB org to a central hub org | C. Use Salesforce-to-Salesforce to connect the orgs and share customer data | D. Implement an Identity Resolution mechanism to unify customer records across orgs in a Data Cloud
column6: For a 360-degree customer view across multiple orgs: Salesforce-to-Salesforce (C) enables native record sharing between Salesforce orgs; Data Cloud with Identity Resolution (D) unifies customer records from multiple orgs in a central platform. Complete Graph (A) is complex and creates tight coupling. Hub-and-Spoke (B) is valid but less specific to native Salesforce tools.

column3: What are the three considerations that the architect should recommend for Change Set deployment? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Change Sets cannot be automated. | B. Change Sets cannot be validated before deployment | C. Change Sets cannot deploy record types | D. Change Sets do not track dependencies | E. Change Sets do not delete components
column6: Change set limitations: cannot be automated via API or CLI (A); do not automatically track or enforce component dependencies (D); and cannot delete components from the target org (E). Change sets CAN be validated before deployment (B is false — Check Only deployments are supported). Change sets CAN deploy record types (C is false).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a huge volume of metadata that cannot be deployed at once. What is the recommended strategy for UC to be successful with the deployment?
column4: A. Use a combination of the Ant migration tool and change sets for deployment. | B. Sort and group the metadata alphabetically and deploy them in the same order. | C. Use a continuous integration tool such as Jenkins to deploy in the right order. | D. Identify metadata dependencies, create logical groupings, and deploy in the right order.
column6: For a large volume of metadata, identifying component dependencies and creating logical deployment groups deployed in dependency order is the recommended strategy. Alphabetical sorting (B) ignores dependencies and causes failures. Jenkins alone (C) does not solve ordering logic. Mixing Ant and change sets (A) adds unnecessary complexity.

column3: An architect is working on a project that relies on functionality that cannot be deployed via the Metadata API. What is the best practice for making sure these components are deployed successfully?
column4: A. Document deployment steps for any components that cannot be automatically deployed | B. Utilize the metadata API's deployAllComponents call | C. Generate and install a managed package that enables the required settings | D. Generate and deploy a change set that enables the required settings
column6: Documenting the manual deployment steps for components that cannot be automatically deployed via Metadata API ensures those components are consistently and correctly handled with each release. This is the Salesforce-documented best practice for handling non-automatable metadata.

column3: A year has passed since a project has gone live and a developer is looking to make an update to an existing Apex class, but is unsure of its purpose. What artifact from the original project should be leveraged to determine the purpose of the class?
column4: A. Test Sign Off Document | B. Test Execution Plan | C. Requirements Traceability Matrix | D. User Acceptance Test Scripts
column6: A Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) links each business requirement to the implementing components (including Apex classes), allowing a developer to trace back the purpose of any class to its originating requirement. Test sign-off (A) and execution plans (B) document testing outcomes. UAT scripts (D) describe user-facing test scenarios.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a customized repository that represents lots of different apps or projects. UC currently is trying to shift from the org development model to the package development model to manage changes. In the org development model, each developer starts their work within their own personal sandbox.When it comes to choosing development environments, what should a Salesforce architect recommend?
column4: A. Keep developing in the dev sandboxes because scratch orgs are not within the code | B. Start using scratch orgs that tracks all of the changes automatically and proceed with a | C. Keep developing in the dev sandboxes, so that the developers feel no impact at all as they | D. Start using scratch orgs because a developer can spin up a scratch org to start a new
column6: When adopting the package development model, developers should use scratch orgs (D) because they are designed for package-based development: they support source tracking, can be spun up quickly from configuration, and align with CI/CD workflows. Continuing in sandboxes (A,C) preserves org development model habits that conflict with the package model.

column3: Universal Containers (\)C) is on the Unlimited Edition of Salesforce orgs. Currently, there are four Developer Pro sandboxes used by dev teams for various purposes, one partial copy sandboxes used for training, one full sandbox used for user acceptance testing. There is another full sandbox used for performance testing during the initial launch, and it is no longer being actively used.A system administrator had reported yesterday that none of the sandboxes can be refreshed.What is the most probable action an architect can help the system administrator with?
column4: A. Create a Salesforce support case, someone should know what is wrong. | B. Contact the Salesforce Account Team to do a sandbox License Count. | C. Delete the full sandbox used for performance testing. | D. It appears the org is using more sandboxes than the license permits. The Partial Copy sandbox shouldn't be used.
column6: Contacting the Salesforce Account Team for a sandbox license count review (B) is the correct first step — they can identify which licenses are allocated, whether limits have been exceeded, and what remediation options are available. Creating a support case (A) is more reactive. Deleting a sandbox without understanding the license situation (C) may not resolve the root cause. Blaming the partial copy sandbox (D) without verification is not actionable.

column3: Which two groups are responsible for the creation and execution of Release Management processes? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. End Users | B. Dev/Build Team | C. Center of Excellence | D. Steering Committee
column6: Release Management processes are created and executed by the Dev/Build Team (B) who implement and package changes, and the Center of Excellence (C) which owns the governance standards and process framework. End Users (A) are consumers of releases. The Steering Committee (D) approves strategic direction but does not execute release management processes.

column3: Universal Containers is reviewing its environment strategy. They have identified a need for a new hotfix environment to resolve any urgent production issues.Which two sandbox types would be appropriate to use as the hotfix environment?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Full sandbox | B. Partial Copy sandbox | C. Developer Pro sandbox | D. Developer sandbox
column6: A Full sandbox (A) provides full production metadata and data, giving the hotfix team a production-equivalent environment to validate fixes. Developer Pro sandbox (C) has sufficient code capacity for code fixes and adequate storage for testing. Partial Copy sandboxes (B) may lack critical production data. Developer sandboxes (D) have very limited storage unsuitable for comprehensive hotfix validation.

column3: A developer on the Universal Containers team has written a test class to test a method that involves a web service callout. Within the test class, the developer is supposed to load test data, create an instance of the Mock object, set the Test.setMock() to that Mock object, call startTest(), execute the code that makes the callout, call stopTest(), and compare the result with expectations. Unfortunately, the Developer forgot to use the Test.setMock() method step.What would happen when the developer runs this test class?
column4: A. The test class fails and the developer will see a message stating: Methods defined as | B. It is impossible to miss the Test.setMock() statement, the Developer Console will not let | C. The test class would make the web service callout and may or may not fail depending on | D. The test class fails without error message since the test class will simply skip the web
column6: Without Test.setMock(), any HTTP callout attempted in a test method will throw a CalloutException with the message 'Methods defined as TestMethod do not support Web service callouts' (A). Salesforce requires all callouts in test context to use mock implementations via Test.setMock().

column3: What are three advantages of using a Source Control system alongside a multi -sandbox development strategy? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Perform code reviews before promoting to a pre -production sandbox | B. Act as a backup in case of catastrophic data loss | C. Automatically deploy changes from sandbox to production | D. Create a branching strategy that tracks each feature or change separately | E. Keep a history of changes made by each developer
column6: Source control benefits alongside multi-sandbox development: code reviews via pull requests before promoting to pre-production sandboxes (A); feature branching to track each change separately (D); and a historical record of which developer made which changes (E). Automatic backups (B) are a secondary benefit. Automatic sandbox-to-production deployment (C) requires additional CI/CD tooling beyond SCM alone.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) had implemented two full sandboxes. One, known as Stage, is used for performance, regression testing, and production readiness check. The other is used primarily for user acceptance testing (UAT). Both full sandboxes were refreshed two months ago. Currently, UC is targeting to start user acceptance testing in two weeks, and do production release in four weeks. An admin also realized Salesforce will have a major release in six weeks.UC needs to release on the current Salesforce version, but also wants to make sure the new Salesforce release does not break anything What should an architect recommend?
column4: A. Refresh Stage from UAT now. After preview cutoff, use the upgraded one for regression | B. Visit trust.salesforce.com to figure out the preview cutoff dates, if the dates had passed, | C. Use the Sandbox Preview Guide to check if there is any necessary action needed. UC | D. Refresh Stage now, and do not refresh UAT. This way, Stage will be on preview and
column6: The Salesforce Sandbox Preview Guide (C) is the official resource for checking preview cutoff dates, understanding which sandboxes will be on the preview release, and planning regression testing. It directly addresses the need to manage deployments against the current release while also validating against the upcoming release.

column3: What are two limitations an architect should consider when designing a strategy for managing technical reference data, with multiple related objects?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Apex CPU limits | B. HTTP response size | C. Circular relationships | D. Depth of nested relationships
column6: Technical reference data limitations with multiple related objects: circular relationships (C) where objects reference each other can cause ordering issues during data loading and deployment; depth of nested relationships (D) can exceed SOQL query relationship depth limits (5 levels). API CPU limits (A) and HTTP response size (B) are less specific to reference data design challenges.

column3: Universal Containers have just acquired Planetary storage. Both companies use salesforce.com to manage their sales activities. The two companies have many customers in common and the company plans to merge the two sales organizations, but the products and sales processes between the two original companies will remain different and distinct. What factor should the company consider in merging the two Salesforce.com orgs into a single org?
column4: A. Customer data could be merged with modifications using standardization and de -duplication | B. Business processes on standard objects can be merged without modifications | C. Transactional sales data could be combined without modification since standard objects are used | D. Salespersons selling both product lines would need two logins, once for each product line
column6: When merging two orgs, customer data can be merged using standardization and de-duplication processes (A). Business processes on standard objects cannot simply be merged without modifications because conflicting business logic must be reconciled (B is false). Transactional sales data requires mapping and transformation (C is false). In a merged single org, users only need one login (D is false — D would be true only for multi-org).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is a high-tech company using SFDX tools and methodologies for its Salesforce development. T UC has moved some of its code and configuration to Unlocked Packages.Which two best practices should an architect recommend to support UC's new package development strategy?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Move everything in the existing codebase to a single monolithic package. | B. Version control does not need to be used, as packages manage all the code and configuration. | C. Test developed packages in test environments before installing to production. | D. Consult the metadata coverage report to identify features supported by packages.
column6: Unlocked package best practices: test developed packages in test environments (sandboxes/scratch orgs) before installing to production (C) to validate functionality; consult the Metadata Coverage Report (D) to identify which metadata types are supported by packages. Version control is still needed alongside packages (B is wrong). A single monolithic package (A) defeats the modular purpose of unlocked packages.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) maintains its Salesforce org using its internal tools and processes for managing its application lifecycle. The UC team has been facing challenges on their development processes in their recent two releases. The architect has recommended the UC team to follow the org development model to address the challenges faced.Which two characteristics of the org development model will help UC address the challenges?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Automated deployment | B. Automated sandbox provisioning | C. Automated defect fixing | D. Automated change tracking
column6: The org development model helps teams with: automated deployment (A) using CLI or Metadata API tools instead of manual change sets; and automated change tracking (D) using source tracking in scratch orgs or enabled sandboxes to identify what has changed. Sandbox provisioning (B) is not automated in the org development model. Automated defect fixing (C) is not a feature of any development model.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses a managed package to install an internal Sales app in five orgs. Within each org there are integrations and local processes with the objects of the managed package. UC wants to use unlocked packages for better integration with CI/CD processes.What would the Salesforce architect recommend for this migration?
column4: A. Migrate all classes, Visualforce, and components from the managed package to the | B. Export all data from objects/fields of the managed package, uninstall it, install the | C. Export all data from objects/fields of the managed package, uninstall it, install the | D. Do not change to unlocked package as it is possible to perform all CI/CD processes with
column6: The correct migration path from managed to unlocked packages: export data from the managed package objects, uninstall the managed package, install the unlocked package (with equivalent structure), and reimport the data (C). This preserves data integrity. Simply migrating code files (A) ignores data. Option B has an incorrect installation sequence.

column3: The opportunity Service and opportunity Service Test classes are in package A but are used only in package B. Both second-generation packages have the same namespace. Therefore, they should be moved to package B for better organization and control.What should the architect recommend for this process?
column4: A. Set the classes as deprecated in package A and recreate them in package B. | B. Set the classes as deprecated in package A and recreate them in package B with new names. | C. Move the classes of package A to package B and create new package versions. | D. Move the classes of package A to package 8 and change the code for package B that called this class from package A.
column6: Moving the classes from package A to package B and updating the calling code in package B (D) is correct — the classes are moved to their logical home, new versions are created for both packages, and references are updated. Deprecating and recreating (A,B) leaves orphaned deprecated classes in package A. Option C omits the critical step of updating the calling code.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) currently uses the org development model and utilizes the Salesforce CLI as the deployment tool. After the feature release artifact (a .zip file) has been tested in a lower sandbox, it is being deployed to the full sandbox for performance testing and production deployment readiness check-Since quick deployment options are not being used, what is the correct way to deploy the artifact to the full sandbox?
column4: A. Authorize to the Full sandbox org; Validate with sfdx:mdapi:deploy; On successful validation, deploy with sfdx:source:deploy | B. Authorize to the Full sandbox org; Validate with sfdx:source:deploy; On successful validation, deploy with sfdx:source:deploy | C. Authorize to the Full sandbox org; Validate with sfdx:mdapi:deploy; On successful validation, deploy with sfdx:mdapi:deploy | D. Authorize to the Full sandbox; validate with sfdx: source: deploy; On successful validation, deploy with sfdx:mdapi:deploy
column6: For a .zip artifact (Metadata API/mdapi format), the correct flow is: authorize the org, validate using sfdx:mdapi:deploy (which handles the zip format), then on successful validation deploy using sfdx:mdapi:deploy (C). Using sfdx:source:deploy (A,B,D options) is for source-format projects, not zip/mdapi format artifacts. Options A and D mix incompatible tools.

column3: Universal Containers is working on the next phase of development for their Salesforce implementation involving a large amount of custom development. Which two strategies should be considered to address a critical production issue occurring in the middle of development? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create separate branches for current development and production bug fixes and deploy the fix with current development when ready | B. Utilize one branch for both development and production bug fixes to avoid out-of-sync branches and simplify deployment | C. Refresh a sandbox for replication of the issue and testing the use -case scenarios once the code is fixed | D. Utilize a source control system to allow separate branches for current development and production bug fixes
column6: For a critical production issue during active development: use a separate branch and a dedicated sandbox (from source control) to fix and test the production bug independently (D); and refresh a sandbox to replicate the production issue and validate the fix before deployment (C). Mixing the hotfix with ongoing development (A) delays the fix. A single branch approach (B) creates merge complexity.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is implementing Service Cloud for their contact centers for 3000 users. They have ~10 million customers. The average speed response time expected is less than 5 seconds with 1,500 concurrent users. What type of testing will help UC measure the page response time?
column4: A. Load testing. | B. System Integration Testing. | C. Unit Testing. | D. Stress Testing.
column6: Load testing (A) measures page response time and throughput under the expected concurrent user count (1,500 users) to determine if the 5-second SLA is met under normal peak conditions. Stress testing (D) tests beyond normal limits to find breaking points. SIT (B) validates integration flows. Unit testing (C) tests code logic in isolation.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) works with different partners and has few admin resources that take care of the day-to-day deployment tasks. As a result, UC would like to find a way to automate the deployments using Metadata API. Which two limitations of Metadata API should be considered when using Metadata API-based Deployments? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Deploy up to 10,000 files, but retrieve more than 10,000 files. | B. Maximum Size of deployed .zip file is 39MB. | C. Maximum size of deployed .zip file is 400MB | D. Deploy and retrieve up to 10,000 files at once.
column6: Metadata API deployment limitations: maximum deployed .zip file size is 39MB (B); deploy and retrieve up to 10,000 files in a single call (D). The claim that you can retrieve more than 10,000 files (A) is incorrect for a single API call. The 400MB limit (C) is incorrect — the correct limit is 39MB for deployment.

column3: Which two environments are appropriate for creating a managed package? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Production Org with LMA | B. Partner Developer Edition Org | C. Developer Pro Sandbox Org | D. Developer Edition Org
column6: Managed packages can only be created in Developer Edition orgs. Partner Developer Edition (B) is a Developer Edition with enhanced limits provided to Salesforce partners for app development. Standard Developer Edition (D) is the baseline environment for managed package creation. Production orgs with LMA (A) are for publishing/distributing packages. Developer Pro/Developer sandboxes (C) cannot host managed packages.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has been using Salesforce Sales Cloud for many years following a highly customized, single-org strategy with great success so far.What two reasons can justify a change to a multi-org strategy?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Acquired company that has its own Salesforce org and operates in a different business | B. UC follows a unification enterprise architecture operating model by having orgs with the | C. UC wants to use Chatter for collaboration among different business units and stop | D. UC is launching a new line of business with independent processes and adding any new
column6: Valid reasons to switch to multi-org: an acquired company operating in a different business domain with its own Salesforce org (A) — keeping them separate may be practical; launching a new independent business line where any new additions would impact the existing org (D). Using Chatter (C) and unified architecture (B) argue for staying on a single org.

column3: Universal Containers is planning to release simple configuration changes and enhancements to their Sales Cloud. A Technical Architect recommend using change sets. Which two advantages would change sets provide in this scenario? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. A simple and declarative method for deployment. | B. The ability to track changes to component. | C. The ability to deploy a very large number of components easily. | D. An easy way to deploy related components.
column6: Change set advantages for simple config/enhancement releases: simple and declarative deployment method (A) requiring no scripting or CLI knowledge; and easy deployment of related components (D) since change sets maintain component relationships automatically. Change sets do not track changes (B is wrong). Deploying large numbers of components is a weakness of change sets (C is wrong).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is working on a project to support environmental cleanup projects with specially designed containers. To support this project, UC is developing a portal for regulatory agencies to use for tracking and reporting of the containers, and these regulatory requirements are well-defined. Many non -regulatory requirements have not been defined yet. The project is on a strict budget and timeline. Which two approaches should UC consider to meet regulatory requirements and to satisfy the needs of end users? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Initiate a waterfall project by gathering the remaining requirements and completing the architecture and design. Initiate the build/test processes with frequent reviews by the stakeholders. On build completion, perform acceptance testing and validate compliance with regulatory requirements | B. Initiate an Agile project based on the known requirements, begin building immediately, and work through remaining requirements as they come up. Budget and timeline will not be a factor with an Agile methodology | C. Initiate an Agile project, beginning with a "sprint 0" to scope and estimate the project and to build the product backlog. Identify the minimum viable product. Initiate building the solution based on the backlog, and co -create the design with the project stakeholders | D. Initiate a waterfall project and start building the features of the solution based on regulatory requirements. In parallel, gather the remaining non -regulatory requirements for the solution, then go back and reconcile the two sets of requirements and re -work the solution as necessary
column6: For regulatory and unknown requirements under tight budget/timeline: a waterfall approach for well-defined regulatory requirements with stakeholder reviews and compliance validation (A) addresses the known regulatory needs; an Agile approach starting with Sprint 0 to build a product backlog, identify MVP, and co-create design (C) handles the unknown non-regulatory requirements. Option D wastes effort by reconciling two parallel streams. Option B incorrectly claims budget/timeline won't matter with Agile.

column3: Universal Containers is building a new complex integration to a legacy system. the legacy system is also going through a major upgrade. Senior leadership has committed to the board that the combined programs will be completed on time. What is the risk with this plan?
column4: A. The legacy system team is using an Agile methodology | B. The deadline is scheduled during a Salesforce release | C. The project team has decided to use the Waterfall methodology | D. Multiple work -streams with dependencies could impact the go-live
