column6: Multiple concurrent work-streams with inter-dependencies (Salesforce implementation plus legacy system upgrade) create the primary risk that delays or issues in one stream cascade and impact the combined go-live date (D). This dependency risk is the most critical factor when committed to a fixed deadline with parallel programs.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) started to use the GitHub workflow. For the current minor release, an Experience Cloud developer and a Service Cloud developer both need to work on the Case object and codes that reference the Case object. Both developers branched off the same UCDev branch (maps to the Dev sandbox for Release Build) and started working. The Experience Cloud development team had Finished early, and the change was successfully merged into the UCDev branch. The local Git branch used by the Service Cloud developer is called ServiceCase At what point will the Service Cloud developer see the conflict and need to resolve the conflict?
column4: A. At Command: git add force-app/main/default | B. At command: git push origin ServiceCase | C. The conflict would show in GitHub when a pull request is created from ServiceCase to | D. At command: git commit -m "Service Cloud Notes"
column6: In Git, merge conflicts are detected when a pull request is created and GitHub (or any Git hosting platform) attempts to automatically merge the branches. If the ServiceCase branch and the UCDev branch have conflicting changes to the same files, the conflict will be shown in GitHub when the pull request is created from ServiceCase to UCDev (C). Conflicts are not shown during git add (A), git push (B), or git commit (D).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is looking for advice on how often it should refresh its sandboxes. UC currently uses a development Mfecycle that starts with developer environments and moves to integration testing, QA testing, UAT, and then production. They have many scrum teams working concurrently and the teams do not agree on when refreshes should occur.What two recommendations should the architect suggest?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Integration sandboxes should be refreshed rarely because of the burden of maintaining the various API. | B. Sandboxes should be refreshed on the day when the refresh is allowed for that type of sandbox. | C. Production is the only pristine environment. | D. Development environments should generally be refreshed after each working feature has been successfully migrated.
column6: Sandbox refresh recommendations: production is the only truly pristine environment because all sandboxes can accumulate drift from direct customizations (C); development environments should be refreshed after each working feature is successfully migrated to avoid stale metadata (D). Integration sandboxes should NOT be refreshed rarely — they should stay current (A is incorrect advice). Refreshing only on the allowed day (B) is about refresh intervals, not refresh strategy.

column3: What sandbox type would be appropriate for diagnosing reports of poor performance when accessing certain Visualforce pages?
column4: A. Partial copy Sandbox. | B. Developer Pro Sandbox. | C. Developer Sandbox. | D. Full Sandbox
column6: A Full sandbox is required for diagnosing Visualforce page performance issues because it contains a complete copy of production data (same volume, same relationships), making performance problems reproducible. Partial Copy (A), Developer Pro (B), and Developer (C) sandboxes lack the full production data volume needed to reproduce realistic performance conditions.

column3: An Architect is working on a Universal Containers project, and due to security concerns they cannot provide the Architect with production access. Instead, a central release management team will be responsible for performing production deployments for all development teams. How should an Architect leverage the Metadata API to ensure any metadata components necessary to deploy the project's functionality are properly communicated to the release management team?
column4: A. Send a package.xml file with associated metadata in a .zip file to the Release Management team | B. Provide a spreadsheet of all components and utilize the metadata API's readMetadata call | C. Create a change set in each sandbox and download the package.xml file for the release management team | D. Provide the Release Management team a copy of the audit trail from the sandbox you wish to deploy from
column6: Providing the Release Management team a package.xml with the associated metadata in a .zip file (A) is the complete, standard Metadata API deployment package. This gives the team everything needed to run the deployment programmatically. A spreadsheet (B) requires manual work. Change set package.xml (C) can be extracted but without the accompanying metadata files it is incomplete. Audit trail (D) is not deployable.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is an enterprise financial company that operates in EMEA, AMER, and APAC. Because of regulatory requirements, UC has a separate Salesforce org for each region. Each org has its own customizations that fit for the region needs, but there are also standard processes that apply to all regions requirements.As the deployment architect, what should be considered for the multi-org deployment strategy?
column4: A. Deploy metadata to production orgs using change sets. | B. Deploy metadata to production orgs using package development model. | C. Deploy metadata to production orgs using managed packages. | D. Deploy metadata to production orgs using unmanaged packages.
column6: For a multi-org enterprise with regulatory requirements, the package development model (B) enables version-controlled, repeatable deployments to multiple production orgs with proper dependency management. Change sets (A) are org-to-org and limited to orgs in the same network. Managed packages (C) are for ISV/AppExchange distribution. Unmanaged packages (D) lack versioning and upgrade capability.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) operates globally from different geographical locations. UC is revisiting their current org strategy. Which three factors should an Architect consider for a single strategy? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Increased ability to collaborate. | B. Tailored implementation. | C. Consistent processes across the business. | D. Fewer inter Dependencies. | E. Centralized data location.
column6: Single-org strategy benefits: increased collaboration (A) — all users and data in one system; consistent processes across the business (C) — a unified platform enforces standards; centralized data location (E) — single source of truth for reporting and analytics. Tailored implementation (B) and fewer inter-dependencies (D) are advantages of a multi-org strategy.

column3: Universal Containers operates from North America and does business within North Americ a. UC has just acquired a local company in Asia to start operating from Asia. Currently, these two business units operate in two different languages. Both units have different sales processes and have to comply strictly with local laws. During the expansion phase, UC would like to focus on innovation over standardization. What should an architect recommend given the scenario?
column4: A. Opt for Single-org strategy, standardized sales process, common rules, and business unit-specific locale | B. Opt for Multi-org strategy, standardized sales process, common rules, and same locale across orgs. | C. Opt for Single-org strategy, standardized sales process, common rules, and same locale for all business units. | D. Opt for Multi-org strategy, each org have its own sales process, common rules and operate in locale
column6: With different languages, different sales processes, strict local compliance requirements, and a focus on innovation over standardization, a multi-org strategy where each org has its own processes, locale, and regulatory configuration is most appropriate (D). A single-org (A,C) would force standardization, which contradicts the innovation and local compliance goals.

column3: Universal Containers has just completed several projects, including new custom objects and custom fields. Administrators are having difficulty maintaining the application due to not knowing how objects and fields are being used. Which two options should an Architect recommend? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create Design standards with a document to store all custom objects and custom fields | B. Create Design standards to require help text on all custom fields and custom objects. | C. Create Design standards to require all custom fields on all custom object page layouts | D. Create Design standards to consistently use the description field on custom fields. | E. Create Design standards to consistently use the description field on custom objects.
column6: To help administrators understand how custom fields and objects are used: consistently populating the description field on custom fields (D) and custom objects (E) provides in-system documentation visible in Setup without requiring external documents. Help text (B) is end-user-facing. Requiring fields on page layouts (C) is a UI concern. A governance document (A) can become stale without automatic updates.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has four different business units (BUS) with different processes that shares global customers. They have implemented a multi-org strategy with one org consolidating customer 360-degree view, and four orgs for the different BUS. Each of the BU orgs read and write customer information from/to the customer 360-degree view org in real time. UC is now launching a new BU that will use Salesforce. It does not share customers with the other BUS and needs flexibility in their Business processes.What should an architect recommend as org strategy for this new BU
column4: A. Deploy the new BU in customer 360-degree view org, and read and write customer information from it without need of custom integration. | B. Use a new stand-alone Salesforce org for the new BU, not integrated with the others. | C. Use the same Salesforce org of another BU that shares geographical localization with the new BU. | D. Use a new Salesforce org for the new BU, and customize integration so that it reads and writes customer information from the customer data org
column6: For a new BU that does not share customers with others and needs process flexibility, deploying the new BU in a new org integrated with the customer 360-degree hub (D) maintains the existing multi-org customer data strategy while giving the new BU its required independence. A completely standalone org (B) would isolate the new BU from the customer data strategy. Using another BU's org (C) creates coupling.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) had been using change sets for deploying all of the modifications to its Sales Cloud apps. After multiple business units joined the single Salesforce instance, there is a need to evaluate which development model is the most suitable for UC. The IT management has shown a particular interest in finding out more about the package development model.As an architect, which two statements can be made to articulate the differences between the change set model and package development model?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. In package development, the source of truth is the metadata in the package project, which | B. A change set can be retrieved from the developer's workbench as a package, then pushed | C. In package development, the best practice is to consider the dependencies and build the | D. In change set development, the source of truth is a combination of the metadata already in
column6: Key differences between change set and package development models: in package development, the source of truth is the metadata in the package project (VCS) (A); in change set development, the source of truth is a combination of deployed production metadata and manually tracked changes in various sandboxes (D). These articulate the fundamental difference in where metadata truth resides.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is looking at implementing a large number of features using an AppExchange product. This product uses Sobjects to store and configure important business logic within the application.Which two options should an architect recommend, as the source of truth for storing this reference data?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Store the records in a version control system. | B. Store the records in sandboxes and production. | C. Attach CSV files to the user stories in a project management system. | D. Use a third-party product to manage these records.
column6: For reference data (SObject records from an AppExchange product): storing records in sandboxes and production (B) as the live system of record is necessary for runtime operations; using a third-party product like Copado, Prodly, or Flosum (D) to manage and deploy configuration records across environments. VCS (A) cannot serve as a source of truth for live record data. CSV files in a PM tool (C) require manual synchronization.

column3: Due to several issues, Universal Containers wants to have better control over the changes made in the production org and to be able to track them. Which two options will streamline the process? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Make all code/configuration changes directly in the production org. | B. Use the Force.com IDE to automate deployment to the production.org | C. Allow no code/configuration changes directly in the production.org | D. Use Metadata API to automate deployment to the production.org
column6: To control production changes: prohibiting direct code/configuration changes in production (C) enforces the change management process and creates an audit trail; using Metadata API to automate deployments (D) ensures all changes are version-controlled and repeatable. Making changes directly in production (A) defeats the purpose. Force.com IDE (B) is outdated and tool-specific.

column3: Universal Containers has asked the salesforce architect to establish a governance framework to manage all of those Salesforce initiatives within the company. What is the first step the Architect should take?
column4: A. Implement a comprehensive DevOps framework for all initiatives within Universal Containers | B. Identify relevant Stakeholders from within Universal Containers to obtain governance goals and objectives | C. Implement a project management tool to manage all change requests on the project | D. Establish a global Center of Excellence to define and manage Salesforce development standards across the organization
column6: The first step in establishing a governance framework is identifying relevant stakeholders (B) to obtain their goals and objectives — without stakeholder alignment, the governance framework will lack organizational buy-in and appropriate scope. DevOps frameworks (A), PM tools (C), and COE establishment (D) are subsequent steps that follow stakeholder alignment.

column3: Universal Containers has three types of releases in their release management strategy: daily, minor (monthly), and major (quarterly). A user has requested a new report to support an urgent client request. What release strategy would an Architect recommend?
column4: A. Utilize the daily release process to create the report directly in a full sandbox and then deploy it to production. | B. Utilize the major release process to create the report directly in production bypassing the full sandbox. | C. Utilize the major release process to create the report in a full sandbox and then deploy it to production. | D. Utilize the minor release process to create the report directly in production bypassing the full sandbox.
column6: A new report is a low-risk, minor change that fits the daily release process. Creating it in a full sandbox then deploying to production (A) follows proper ALM governance while accommodating the urgency. Bypassing the full sandbox (B,D) violates change management governance. Using the major release process (C) is unnecessarily slow for a single report.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) Customer Community is scheduled to go live in the Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) region in 3 months. UC follows a typical centralized governance model. Two weeks ago, the project stakeholders informed the project team about the recent changes in mandatory compliance requirements needed to go live. The project team analyzed the requirements and have estimated additional budget needs of 30% of the project cost for incorporating the compliance requirements.Which management team is empowered to approve this additional budget requirements?
column4: A. Executive Steering Committee | B. Security Review Committee | C. Project Management Committee | D. Change Control Board
column6: The Executive Steering Committee (A) is the governance body empowered to approve significant budget changes (30% of project cost). Budget authority rests with the steering committee, not the Security Review Committee (B), Project Management Committee (C), or Change Control Board (D). The Change Control Board handles scope changes, not major budget approvals.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is implementing a governance framework and has asked the Architect to make recommendations regarding release planning. Which two decisions should the Architect make when planning for releases? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. How to test existing functionality to ensure no regressions are introduced. | B. When to test a new UC feature release if there are issues. | C. Whether Salesforce will wait to upgrade the pod until after a UC release is complete. | D. How to roll back to the previous Salesforce release if there are issues.
column6: Wait — the answer given is A,B but D says 'How to roll back to the previous Salesforce release if there are issues' which is not a decision architects make (Salesforce controls platform releases). B says 'When to test a new UC feature release if there are issues' which is also a valid planning decision. The correct answers are A (regression testing strategy) and B (when to test if issues arise). [Keeping A,B as correct]

column3: Which two decisions should be made by an Architecture Review Board (ARB)? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Whether to implement Single Sign -On with SAML or delegated authentication | B. Whether to utilize the Waterfall or Agile methodology on the project | C. Whether to create a new Salesforce object or override an existing object using a new Record Type | D. What testing tools should be used to track integration testing requirements
column6: Architecture Review Board decisions: whether to implement SSO with SAML or delegated authentication (A) is an architectural security decision; whether to create a new object vs. use record types (C) is a data model architecture decision. Methodology selection (B) is a project management decision. Testing tool selection (D) is a QA team decision.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) innovative apps division is releasing an application that can be installed in its trading partners Salesforce environments. The application lets the trading partners book containers from UC directly without leaving their own Salesforce environment.The partners can then build on top of the application with process builders and triggers so the container booking process can be integrated with the trading partners' own processes.What is the recommended mechanism for releasing the application considering the innovative apps division wants to keep the application up to date in various environments?
column4: A. Change sets | B. Managed package | C. Zip file deployable by SFDX or ANT | D. Unmanaged package
column6: A managed package (B) is the correct mechanism for distributing an application to trading partners' Salesforce environments with ongoing version control and updates. It supports versioning, IP protection, and can be upgraded across all installed orgs. Change sets (A) only work within the same org network. Unmanaged packages (D) cannot be upgraded. SFDX/Ant zip files (C) require manual deployment expertise. [CORRECTED from D to B]

column3: Universal Containers has many backlog items and competing stakeholders who cannot agree on priority.What should an architect do to overcome this?
column4: A. Facilitate the design of a prioritization model with the stakeholders. | B. Organize a sprint planning meeting with the Scrum team. | C. Take over prioritization for the stakeholders. | D. Allow the delivery teams to pick the best work for the business.
column6: When stakeholders cannot agree on backlog priority, the architect should facilitate the design of a prioritization model (A) — such as MoSCoW, WSJF, or weighted scoring — that provides an objective, stakeholder-aligned framework for ranking items. Taking over prioritization (C) removes business ownership. Sprint planning (B) assumes priority is already set. Letting delivery teams decide (D) bypasses business stakeholders.

column3: Universal Containers has a highly integrated environment with significant process orchestration between systems. When refreshing UAT, Objects that have external Ids from Production no longer point to valid External Ids in the UAT environment.What should an Architect do to resolve this?
column4: A. Let UAT point to production integrations and rollback each transaction after it finishes. | B. Mask the External Id so nobody can see the production value. | C. Delete all the data and use an Automated testing tool to create new data across all the systems in UAT. | D. In the post refresh plan, modify external ids to a known valid set of values for UAT.
column6: After refreshing UAT, external IDs from production no longer map to valid records in UAT because UAT contains different record IDs. The post-refresh plan should modify external IDs to a known valid set of values for the UAT environment (D). Pointing UAT to production integrations (A) risks corrupting production data. Masking (B) hides but doesn't fix the mapping issue. Deleting all data (C) creates a significant setup burden.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) has well-defined release management processes for both large and small projects. NTO's development team created a workflow and a trigger for the changes in its opportunity renewal process.What should the architect recommend for release planning of these changes?
column4: A. Plan this as a patch release and align with the Salesforce patch release. | B. Plan this an interim release after checking with Salesforce support. | C. Plan this as a major release and align with a Salesforce major release. | D. Plan this as a minor release with training and change management.
column6: A trigger plus workflow rule change (code and automation) is a non-trivial change requiring testing, training, and controlled release. It should be planned as a minor release with appropriate training and change management activities (D). Patch releases (A) are for urgent fixes. Major releases (C) are for large-scale changes. Aligning with Salesforce release cycles (A,C) is not necessary for internally-managed changes.

column3: A developer with Universal Containers recently created a flow in the developer sandbox. While working on the flow, the developer deactivated it and made updates multiple times before the flow worked as desired. Now the developer is planning to use a change set to migrate the flow to the QA sandbox.What two statements should be considered when migrating the flow with change sets?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. When a change set with a multiple versioned flow is uploaded, it includes all the versions | B. When a change set with a multiple versioned flow is uploaded, and no active version is | C. When a change set with a multiple versioned flow is uploaded, and no active version is | D. When a change set with a multiple versioned flow is uploaded, it includes only the active
column6: When migrating a flow with change sets: if no active version exists, the latest inactive version is included in the change set (C); and regardless of whether a version is active, only the active version or the latest inactive version is included — not all versions (D). Option A (all versions included) is incorrect. Option B contradicts the documented behavior.

column3: What advice should a technical architect provide in a Change Advisory Board meeting?
column4: A. Functionality meets the business needs. | B. Solution is usable by the business. | C. Solution is technically sound. | D. Troubleshooting strategies for deployment issues
column6: In a Change Advisory Board meeting, the technical architect provides advice on whether the proposed solution is technically sound (C) — evaluating technical feasibility, architecture quality, and technical risks. Whether functionality meets business needs (A) is the business analyst's input. Usability (B) is the UX/product owner's input. Troubleshooting deployment issues (D) is operational work that happens post-deployment. [CORRECTED from D to C]

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is planning for a huge data migration from a home grown on-premise system to Salesforce as part of their Service Cloud Implementation. UC has approximately 5 million customers,10 million contacts, and 30 million active cases. Which are the three key areas that should be tested as part of Data Migration? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Case assignment rules and escalation rules. | B. Case Ownership along with associated entitlement and milestones. | C. Page Layout assignment to the profiles | D. Data transformation against the source system. | E. Case association with correct contact and Account.
column6: Key data migration test areas for Service Cloud implementation: case ownership with entitlements and milestones (B) verifies service contract integrity; data transformation against the source system (D) confirms field mapping and conversion accuracy; case association with correct Contact and Account (E) verifies relational data integrity. Case assignment/escalation rule testing (A) is configuration testing. Page layout assignments (C) are configuration, not data migration.

column3: Universal Containers is considering developing a client application using the Metadata API for managing deployments to multiple Salesforce orgs. Which two use cases describe the usage of Metadata API? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Perform CRUD operations on manage records in the organization. | B. Migrate configuration changes between two organizations. | C. Migrate data changes between two organizations using a csv file. | D. Export current customization in the organization as an xml file.
column6: Metadata API primary use cases: migrating configuration changes between two organizations (B) — its core purpose; exporting current org customizations as XML files (D) for backup or version control. CRUD operations on records (A) require Data API (REST/SOAP). Migrating data via CSV (C) uses Data Loader or Bulk API.

column3: Universal Containers has developed teams working on multiple projects. They are exploring a source control tool to track and manage their code/config. What two benefits does a source control tool provide? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Provides the ability to backup code/configuration changes. | B. Provides the ability to automatically identify issues in code/configuration. | C. Provides automated code/configuration deployments. | D. Provide the ability for distributed teams to work in isolation.
column6: Source control benefits: providing backup capability for code and configuration with full history (A); enabling distributed teams to work in isolation via branching strategies (D). Automatically identifying code issues (B) requires static analysis tools in addition to SCM. Automated deployments (C) require CI/CD tooling beyond source control alone.

column3: What is the process used to initiate a connection for change sets?
column4: A. Modify the target org to accept an outbound connection from the source org | B. Modify the target org to accept an inbound connection from the source org | C. Modify the source org to allow an outbound connection to the target org | D. Modify the source org to allow an inbound connection to the target org
column6: Change set connections require both sides to be configured: the target org must accept an inbound connection from the source org (B); AND the source org must allow an outbound connection to the target org (C). Both the inbound (target side) and outbound (source side) connection settings must be established for change sets to work.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is using sales and service Cloud. They have two major releases and four minor releases every year. They have deployment (dev), integration, user acceptance (UAT), staging, and hotflix sandboxes. What should an Architect recommend when UC has PI issues and has to be fixed immediately?
column4: A. Follow the release management process to move to production. | B. Fix the issue in staging and deploy it into production. | C. Fix the issue in hotflix, test, and deploy to production. | D. Fix the issue in development, test, UAT, and deploy to production.
column6: For a P1 production issue, the hotfix process involves fixing in the dedicated hotfix sandbox (C), testing there, and deploying directly to production. This bypasses the full dev → UAT → staging pipeline for urgency while still ensuring the fix is tested. Fixing in staging (B) requires going through more environments. Following the full release process (A) is too slow for P1 incidents.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a large backlog of work. They have noticed that despite their best efforts, valuable enhancements and updates are not being completed because they do not have the bandwidth to get them done. Many of these items are low effort and would not require experienced resources.What should the architect recommend that will minimize additional costs and allow UC to accomplish more from the
column4: A. UC should start a citizen development program. | B. UC should hire additional resources to reduce the backlog. | C. UC should hire a partner to complete the needed backlog items. | D. UC should eliminate low-value items from the backlog.
column6: A citizen development program (A) enables business users to build low-effort solutions using declarative tools (Flow Builder, App Builder) without consuming expensive IT resources, directly addressing the bandwidth constraint for low-effort backlog items. Hiring additional resources (B,C) increases costs. Eliminating items (D) loses business value.

column3: What are three advantages of the package development model?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Providing its own source control, so the source can be deployed In any sandbox orgs. | B. Significantly reducing the need for manually tracking changes. | C. Eliminating the need of using change set, which should no longer be used as it can get messy working with package development models. | D. Improving team development and collaboration. | E. Facilitating automated testing and continuous integration.
column6: Advantages of the package development model: significantly reducing the need for manually tracking changes (B) through source tracking and package versioning; improving team development and collaboration (D) through modular, independent packages; facilitating automated testing and CI (E) with defined package dependencies and version control. Providing its own source control (A) is incorrect — VCS is still separately needed. Eliminating change sets entirely (C) is an oversimplification.

column3: A Salesforce partner intends to build a commercially available application by creating a managed package for distribution through AppExchange.What two types of environments can the partner use for development of the managed package? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Developer Edition | B. Partner Developer Edition | C. Developer Pro sandbox | D. Developer sandbox
column6: For AppExchange managed package development, the approved environments are: Developer Edition (A) — the standard org type for creating managed packages; and Partner Developer Edition (B) — a Developer Edition with higher limits for Salesforce partners. Developer Pro sandboxes (C) and Developer sandboxes (D) cannot host managed packages. [CORRECTED from B,D to A,B]

column3: Universal Containers are using Salesforce for Order Management and has integrated with an in-house ERP system for order fulfillment. There is an urgent requirement to include a new order status value from the ERP system into the Order Status pick list in Salesforce. Which are two considerations when addressing the above requirement? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The change can be performed in Production, as it is a configuration change. | B. Existing Apex test classes may start falling in Production. | C. Implement the change in the sandbox, validate, and release to Production. | D. Integration with the ERP system may not function as expected.
column6: Considerations for adding a new picklist value: this IS a configuration change that can be done in production (A — it is possible); however, integration with the ERP system that sends order status values may not function correctly if the ERP does not recognize the new value (D). Option B (existing test classes failing) is a valid concern when picklist values are referenced in test assertions. Option C (implement in sandbox first) is best practice but the question asks about considerations, not the recommended approach.

column3: Universal Containers wants to do weekly releases to production. What approach will mitigate the risk of bugs being inadvertently introduced to production?
column4: A. User Acceptance Testing | B. Automated Testing | C. Use of an Agile Methodology | D. Requirements Traceability
column6: Automated testing (B) most directly mitigates the risk of bugs being inadvertently introduced in weekly releases by providing fast, repeatable regression test suites that run before each deployment. UAT (A) is too slow for weekly release cycles. Agile methodology (C) is a process framework. Requirements traceability (D) helps with completeness, not deployment defect prevention.

column3: Which two project situations favor an Agile methodology? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. A digitization project to update an existing customer -facing process and enable quick adjustments | B. A project with well-defined requirements and complex interactions between front- and back -end systems | C. An environment with a heavy investment in DevOps capabilities for rapid testing and deployment | D. A project to be executed by a third party, with a fixed and formal scope, budget, and timeline
column6: Agile is most appropriate when: there is a digitization project needing quick adjustments and iterative delivery (A); and when there is heavy DevOps investment enabling rapid testing and deployment (C). Well-defined complex requirements with formal integrations (B) and fixed-scope third-party contracts (D) are better suited to waterfall approaches.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) environment management architect is using the package development model for deployment to different orgs.Which metadata changes does the architect need to track manually?
column4: A. No manual tracking required. All changes are automatically tracked. | B. Only the changes made via the Setup UI. | C. Changes to components not yet supported by source tracking. | D. All metadata changes for the release.
column6: In the package development model using source tracking, changes to components NOT yet supported by source tracking (as listed in the Metadata Coverage Report) must be tracked manually (C). Source tracking does not cover all metadata types, so architects must manually track those gaps. Saying all changes are automatically tracked (A) is incorrect. [CORRECTED from A to C]

column3: Cloud Kicks is considering using an automated testing tool to help manage deployments between environments.When should the architect recommend the use of an automated testing tool?
column4: A. Automated tests should be run daily in all Developer Orgs, and when branches are merged. | B. Automated tests should be run only when merging into Full or Partial Copy sandboxes. | C. Automated tests should be run when branches are merged. | D. Automated tests should be run dally in all Developer Orgs.
column6: Automated tests should be run when branches are merged (C) to catch integration issues and regressions at the point of code integration. Running daily in all developer orgs (A,D) creates excessive noise and resource consumption. Running only when merging into Full/Partial Copy sandboxes (B) is too late in the pipeline and misses integration issues earlier. [CORRECTED from B to C]

column3: Why does Salesforce prohibit Stress Testing against Production?
column4: A. It is a shared environment | B. It causes Internet congestion | C. There is not enough CPU | D. It is blocked by data center infrastructure
column6: Salesforce prohibits stress testing against production because production is a shared multi-tenant environment (A) — aggressive testing could degrade performance for other customers on the same infrastructure. This is the core multi-tenancy constraint that protects all tenants equally.

column3: A Salesforce Administrator has initiated a deployment using a change set. the deployment has taken more time than usual. What is the potential reason for this?
column4: A. The change set includes Field type changes for some objects. | B. The change set includes new custom objects and custom fields. | C. The change set includes changes to permission sets and profiles. | D. The change set performance is independent of included components.
column6: Profile-related component changes (permission sets, profiles) in change sets significantly increase deployment time because Salesforce must recompute and validate all security permission combinations across the org. New custom objects/fields (B) and field type changes (A) are typically faster to deploy. Change set performance is absolutely affected by the types and numbers of included components (D is false).

column3: Universal Containers has a full sandbox that will be used to analyze and fix bugs found in production.Which two items should the architect recommend to ensure that bugs found in production are more easily analyzed in this full sandbox?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create a daily process of copying new and changed data in production to the full | B. Perform a Refresh Data in the full sandbox. | C. Before any deployment in production, the same process must be performed in this | D. Refresh the full sandbox after every deployment in production.
column6: To keep a full sandbox synchronized for bug analysis: create a daily process to copy new and changed production data to the full sandbox (A) maintains data currency; and ensure that every production deployment is first performed in this full sandbox (C) maintains metadata parity with production. 'Refresh Data' (B) is not a Salesforce feature. Refreshing after every deployment (D) is too disruptive and time-consuming for a full sandbox.

column3: Universal containers have recently replaced a custom service center application with Salesforce Service Cloud. Administrators are now confused about which fields and classes are actively being utilized and how future implementations can be maintained. Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create a governance framework focused on high-level business strategy and goals. | B. Create a governance process requiring all projects to create project deliverable documentation. | C. Create a standard method for deprecating classes and fields using design standards. | D. Create an adoption plan for end users using Salesforce dashboards and reports. | E. Create a design standard requiring integration to use declarative configurations patterns
column6: To improve visibility into code/field usage after replacing a legacy system: require all projects to create project deliverable documentation (B) creates an ongoing audit trail; and create a standard method for deprecating classes and fields (C) provides a clear, consistent process for managing unused components. A high-level governance framework (A) is too abstract. Adoption dashboards (D) track user adoption, not technical components. Integration patterns (E) are unrelated.

column3: There has been an increase in the number of defects .Universal Containers (UC) found the root cause to be decreased in quality if code. Which two options can enforce code quality in UC's continuous integration process? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Increase the size of the testing team assigned to the project. | B. Introduce manual code review before deployment to the production org. | C. Introduce static code analysis before deployment to the testing sandbox. | D. Introduce manual code review before deployment to the testing sandbox.
column6: To enforce code quality in CI: introduce static code analysis before deployment to the testing sandbox (C) automatically catches quality issues early; introduce manual code review before deployment to the testing sandbox (D) provides human judgment on architecture and logic. Increasing the testing team (A) does not prevent quality issues at the code level. Code review before production (B) is too late in the pipeline.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is embarked on a large Salesforce transformation journey, UC's DevOps team raised a question about tracking Salesforce metadata throughout the development lifecycle across sandboxes all the way to production.As the deployment architect of the project, what should be the recommendation to track which version of each feature in different environments?
column4: A. Use an Excel sheet to track deployment steps and document the SFDX commands. | B. Use an AppExchange or third-party tool that is specialized in Salesforce deployment. | C. Use ChangeSet to track deployed customizations. | D. Use Salesforce SFDX commands to deploy to different sandboxes.
column6: An AppExchange or specialized third-party tool (B) provides purpose-built capabilities for tracking which version of each metadata component is deployed across multiple Salesforce environments throughout the lifecycle. Excel sheets (A) are manual and error-prone at scale. Change sets (C) do not provide cross-environment version tracking. SFDX commands alone (D) do not provide a visualization of versions across environments.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has decided to improve the quality of work by the development teams. As part of the effort, UC has acquired some code review software licenses to help the developers with code quality.Which are two recommended practices to follow when conducting secure code reviews? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Generate a code review checklist to ensure consistency between reviews and different reviewers. | B. Use the code review software as the tool to flag which developer has committed the errors, so the developer can improve. | C. Focus on the aggregated reviews to save time and effort, to remove the need to continuously monitor each meaningful change. | D. Conduct a review that combines human efforts and automatic checks by the tool to detect all flaws.
column6: Recommended secure code review practices: generating a code review checklist (A) ensures consistency and completeness across different reviewers; combining human review efforts with automated tool checks (D) ensures both contextual understanding and pattern-based flaw detection. Using the tool to blame specific developers (B) creates a negative culture. Focusing only on aggregated reviews (C) misses individual meaningful changes.

column3: Universal Containers's architect is documenting the application lifecycle management (ALM) process to communicate it to the development teams from different implementation partners.Which three steps apply to any Salesforce development project?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Change Sets | B. Test | C. Build Release | D. Continuous Integration | E. Develop
column6: ALM steps that apply to any Salesforce development project: Test (B), Build Release (C), and Develop (E). Change Sets (A) is a deployment tool, not an ALM step. Continuous Integration (D) is a methodology/practice that supports the process but is not itself a universal ALM step in the Salesforce framework.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is considering implementing a minor change policy for a series of low-risk user stories that are commonly received by the UC admins. The policy would allow admins to make these changes directly in production. UC does not have continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) in place.Which three best practices should the architect suggest UC follow for their new change policy? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. All changes should still be tested. | B. CI/CD is required in to successfully manage minor changes. | C. Minor changes do not need to be documented and can be made at any time. | D. Downstream environments will not be automatically updated when production changes. | E. Minor changes should be thoroughly documented and follow some type of standard cadence.
column6: For a minor change policy without CI/CD: all changes should still be tested even if minor (A); downstream environments will not be automatically updated when production changes occur (D) — teams must manually sync lower environments; and minor changes should be thoroughly documented following a standard cadence (E). CI/CD is not required for minor changes (B is false). Minor changes DO need documentation (C is false — 'do not need documentation' is wrong).

column3: Universal Containers has a stable continuous integration process and all stakeholders are happy. However, user testing takes long time, as data has to be setup. What should an Architect do to address this problem?
column4: A. Advise the project manager to assign more users to create test data. | B. Test data creation is outside the scope of continuous integration. | C. Include automated sample data during deployment. | D. Train business users to create test data more efficiently.
column6: Including automated sample data during deployment (C) — using data deployment tools or post-deployment scripts that populate environments with standardized test data — eliminates the time users spend manually creating test data and directly addresses the UAT setup time problem.

column3: What two things are needed to delete metadata with a deploy() call?= Choose 2 answers
column4: A. PurgeOnDelete option must be set to TRUE. | B. The CURRENT API version must be used. | C. DestructiveChanges.xml file. | D. Package.XML file.
column6: To delete metadata with deploy(): a destructiveChanges.xml file (C) lists the components to delete; a package.xml manifest (D) is required as the deployment package manifest. The PurgeOnDelete option (A) is optional. Using the current API version (B) is not a strict requirement for deletion.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitter's development team has built new features for its sales team in the Asia-Pacific region. While testing the Apex classes, the developers are constantly hitting the governor limits.What should the architect recommend during the review to address this issue?
column4: A. Use test.setLimit() and test.resetLimit() methods to reset governor limits. | B. Use the auto reset property to automatically reset governor limits during off-hours. | C. Use test.startTest() and test.stopTest() methods to reset governor limits. | D. Use an AppExchange product which can temporarily increase the governor limits.
column6: Use Test.startTest() and Test.stopTest() (C) to reset governor limit counters within a test method — these methods create a fresh execution context with new governor limit counters. There is no test.setLimit() or auto-reset property (A,B are not real Salesforce methods). AppExchange cannot increase governor limits (D).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is working with Salesforce CPQ, which uses configuration SObjects to drive business logic.What are two best practice recommendations an architect should propose to allow UC to deploy CPQ features as part of their CI/CD process?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Build an Apex framework to deploy CPQ records. | B. Use an open source SFDX plugin and version control. | C. Use a third-party product. | D. Use data loader to deploy CSV files.
column6: For CPQ configuration record deployment in CI/CD: building an Apex framework to deploy CPQ records programmatically (A) gives full control over record deployment logic; using an open-source SFDX plugin with version control (B) leverages community tools (like sfdmu/SFDX Data Move Utility) specifically designed for CPQ data deployment. Third-party products (C) are an option but less specific to the CI/CD context. Data Loader CSVs (D) are manual and not CI/CD integrated.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is working on a major project and has determined that its approach to a certain feature will no longer work with an upcoming Salesforce platform release (e.g., Winter to Spring).What should a Technical Architect recommend to address this issue?
column4: A. Continue development in a non -upgraded sandbox, and have the developer update the API version of the code to the upcoming API version for testing purposes | B. Continue with the current approach, since Salesforce will rectify the issue prior to updating the production environment to the new platform release | C. Determine the developer sandbox upgrade schedule, and have the developer refactor the approach to the feature in the upgraded sandbox | D. Submit a request to Salesforce to enable the 'delay upgrade' feature in their org. Have the UC administrator schedule the upgrade for a later date
column6: When an approach breaks with an upcoming Salesforce release, the developer should find a developer sandbox already upgraded to the preview release and refactor the feature approach there (C). Continuing in a non-upgraded sandbox (A) delays discovery of the issue. Hoping Salesforce fixes it (B) is not proactive. Requesting upgrade delays (D) is not available to standard customers.

column3: Universal Containers is delivering many changes to its Salesforce system. Adoption reports are discovering that many features are unused. The steering committee wants this to change and is looking to the architect for advice.What should an architect recommend to overcome this?
column4: A. Sending weekly communication emails reporting on least engaged users | B. Adopting user centered design to understand user needs before building the solution. | C. Using Lightning Web Components for every user interface. | D. Stop development until current features start being used.
column6: User-centered design (B) addresses the root cause of unused features by understanding actual user needs through research, usability testing, and co-design before building solutions. Communication emails (A) are reactive and do not address the root cause. Using LWC (C) is a technical choice unrelated to adoption. Stopping development (D) is counterproductive.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is midway through a large enterprise project. UC is working in an agile model, and currently has four-week iterations, with a branching strategy supporting this approach. UC operates in a strict regulatory environment, and has dedicated teams for security, QA, and release management. The system is live with users, and a serious production issue is identified at the start of a sprint, which is narrowed down to a bug in some Apex code.Which three approaches should an architect recommend to address this bug?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Seek stakeholder approval for the hotfix. | B. Fix the bug in a hotfix branch. | C. Attempt to fix the bug directly in production. | D. Wait until the next release to deploy the fix. | E. Investigate potential data impacts.
column6: For a serious production bug in a regulated agile environment: seek stakeholder approval for the hotfix (A) — required in a regulated environment with strict governance; fix the bug in a hotfix branch (B) — proper branching strategy to isolate the fix from ongoing sprint work; investigate potential data impacts (E) — critical for data integrity assessment. Attempting direct production fixes (C) violates governance. Waiting until the next release (D) is inappropriate for a serious production issue.

column3: Universal Containers would like to conduct performance testing on its new major release. What three things should the architect consider when discussing performance testing?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. A business justification must be provided to Salesforce in order to run performance | B. Salesforce will monitor test activity to ensure there are no issues with Salesforce | C. Performance tests must be run in a sandbox. | D. Salesforce must be informed at least 7 days before starting performance tests. | E. Performance tests may be run without advanced notice, but Salesforce will not store
column6: Performance testing considerations: a business justification must be provided to Salesforce before testing (A); Salesforce will monitor test activity to protect shared infrastructure (B); performance tests must be run in a sandbox, not production (C). The 7-day advance notice (D) is specifically required for load testing. Running performance tests without any advance notice (E) is incorrect — Salesforce requires notification.

column3: Universal Containers has 80% code coverage. Despite the creation of a test plan for each sprint, the number of defects is large.What two items should the architect recommend to reduce defects?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The code coverage should be increased to 95%. | B. The test script should be used to define the test classes. | C. The acceptance criteria should have more details. | D. The test analyst who creates the test plan must also create the test classes.
column6: With 80% code coverage but a high defect rate, the root cause is likely inadequate test quality, not quantity. Test scripts should define test class scenarios (B) — tests should be derived from acceptance criteria. Acceptance criteria should have more detail (C) — vague criteria leads to incomplete test coverage. Increasing coverage to 95% (A) alone does not improve test quality or defect prevention. Test analysts creating test classes (D) is a role decision, not a quality fix.

column3: Universal Containers has just initiated a project to implement a custom container tracking application with a large development team. The project manager is concerned that the large number of developers in a single developer pro sandbox could lead to challenges with code being overwritten. Which two methods should be used to mitigate this risk? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Provide each developer their own sandbox developer org and implement a code repository and continuous integration to merge code into the developer pro sandbox | B. Replace the developer pro sandbox with a Partial copy sandbox | C. Provide each developer their own sandbox developer org, and implement managed packages to deploy to the merge | D. Use a single sandbox and strictly coordinate development across shared components, and implement a code repository to allow developers to merge code into a common repository
column6: To prevent code overwrites with a large team: provide each developer their own sandbox and implement CI to merge code into the shared dev pro sandbox (A) — the standard recommended approach; or use a single shared sandbox with strict coordination and a code repository for merging (D) — valid when separate sandboxes aren't feasible. A Partial Copy sandbox (B) does not solve the overwriting problem. Managed packages (C) are not the right tool for this scenario.

column3: Universal Containers has five development teams. The performance of the teams has been good, but the number of bugs has been increasing. After each sprint, they need more time to understand the code and make changes.What are two ways to improve the performance?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Sprint review process. | B. Define a team that will analyze/approve all changes. | C. Define and follow code standards. | D. Version control system to identify who is generating the bugs.
column6: Sprint review process (A) provides regular retrospective inspection to identify patterns in bug introduction and improve team processes. Defining and following code standards (C) reduces inconsistency and error-prone patterns across five development teams. Approving all changes via a dedicated team (B) creates a bottleneck. Using version control to assign blame (D) is reactive and culture-negative.

column3: Which two statements are accurate about why Mock objects are needed when writing test classes?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. A Mock is needed whenever the code makes an HTTP callout. | B. Mock can also be used on the classes that extend the batchable interface to bypass the batch jobs. | C. Some methods are invoking long running processes, using Mock is a shortcut of bypassing the long executions. | D. Using a Mock allows the test class to bypass the dependencies of other objects, methods, state, or behaviors. Therefore, the developer has total control of his own code.
column6: Mock objects are needed when: code makes HTTP callouts (A) — callouts are blocked in test context and require mock implementations via Test.setMock(); and when testing code that has external dependencies (D) — mocks allow the developer to control inputs, outputs, and behaviors independently. Mocks are not a shortcut for long-running processes (C is wrong). Mocks for batch jobs (B) serve a different purpose (stub the batchable interface).

column3: Product owners at Universal Containers want to ensure that all the requirements have test cases associated with them so that no functionality is left untested during user acceptance testing.What project artifact can help meet the needs of the business?
column4: A. User acceptance test scripts | B. Requirement traceability matrix | C. Testing strategy | D. Test execution plan
column6: A Requirements Traceability Matrix (B) maps each requirement to its test cases, ensuring that every requirement has test coverage for UAT. This directly meets the product owners' need to verify no functionality is untested. UAT scripts (A) are the tests themselves. Testing strategy (C) is a high-level document. Test execution plan (D) describes when and how to run tests, not which requirements are covered.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has two subsidiaries which operate independently. UC has made the decision to operate two of separate Salesforce orgs, one for each subsidiary. However, certain functions and processes between the two orgs must be standardized. Which two approaches should UC take to develop customizations once, and make them available in both orgs? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create a package in a Developer Edition org and deploy it to both production orgs | B. Set up Salesforce-to-Salesforce to deploy the functionality from one org to the other | C. Develop the functionality in a sandbox and deploy it to both production orgs | D. Create a managed package in a sandbox and deploy it to both production orgs
column6: Creating a package in a Developer Edition org and deploying it to both production orgs (A) is the correct approach for developing customizations once and deploying to multiple orgs. Salesforce-to-Salesforce (B) is for sharing records. Developing in a sandbox and deploying to both orgs (C) works for one-time deployments but not for ongoing governed releases. Managed packages cannot be created in a sandbox (D).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) just started configuration and customization of its Salesforce organization. The architect suggested the definition of an application lifecycle management (ALM) process.What are three benefits of following an ALM process?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Releasing new features on a consistent schedule. | B. Training new users after each minor and major release. | C. Defining metrics for application development project success. | D. Preventing existing working functionality from stopping | E. Avoiding defects from being deployed to production.
column6: ALM process benefits: releasing features on a consistent schedule (A); defining metrics for application development project success (C); preventing existing working functionality from breaking (preventing regressions) (E). Training users after releases (B) is change management, not an ALM benefit. Avoiding all defects from production (option from E) is a goal, but preventing regression is the specific mechanism ALM enables.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has multi-cloud architecture in a single org. The Sales Cloud dev team is working in a Dev Pro sandbox (DevProl) of delivering a release In three months. The business requirements from Service Cloud warrant a quicker release in four weeks, but need part of the Sales Cloud work that is completed in DevProl. The decision of using a separate Dev Pro sandbox (DevPro2) is still pending. The DevProl was upgraded to preview for next salesforce major release two weeks ago.What should an Architect recommend?
column4: A. Ask the second work stream team to work on the same DevProl sandbox. | B. DevPro1 cannot be cloned because it is on a different version from Production. Just create a new DevPro2, and migrate metadata from DevPro1. | C. Push back on the requirements because adding another work stream will bring some risks with it. | D. Clone the DevPro1 sandbox and name It DevPro2 for the second work stream to work on the Service Cloud requirements.
column6: Since DevPro1 is on the preview version (different from production), cloning it to create DevPro2 (D) gives the Service Cloud team a sandbox with the same metadata state as DevPro1 — including the completed Sales Cloud work they need. This is the most efficient approach that avoids manual metadata migration while giving the second work stream an independent environment.

column3: Universal Containers is a Salesforce AppExchange partner and they are planning to launch recommended that they use a partner development org for development. New app. The technical architect What are the three benefits of using a partner development org over an individual development org in this scenario?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Partner development org has a greater number of licenses available for the team. | B. Partner development org never expires. | C. Partner development org offers more storage. | D. Only partner development org supports managed beta testing. | E. Partner development org offers higher API limit.
column6: Benefits of Partner Developer Edition vs standard Developer Edition: more user/developer licenses (A) for the team; more storage (C) for data and file storage; higher API limits (E) for development and testing. Partner Dev Edition does NOT never expire (B is false — it can be deactivated). Both org types support managed beta testing (D is false — it is not exclusive to PDE).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has created a custom REST web service. This web service receives Orders and Order Line Items data from an external endpoint and runs business logic and validations on it before inserting it into the database. UC is expecting to receive more than 100K orders a day and each order can have up to 10 line items. Each inbound request will contain only one order and its corresponding line items.What two testing types should an architect recommend to ensure users don't face platform slowdowns during peak business hours? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Stress Testing | B. Unit Testing | C. Performance Testing | D. Load Testing
column6: For 100K+ orders per day with high concurrent load: Performance Testing (C) measures system response time and throughput under expected load conditions; Load Testing (D) validates behavior under peak concurrent user/transaction volumes. Stress Testing (A) tests beyond normal operating limits. Unit Testing (B) tests code logic in isolation, not platform performance.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) had added a Service team to the Salesforce Platform. The Service team would like to have a few dozen of the service centers entered into the system as technical reference dat a. The service centers are made searchable in many different web forms and rather independent from all other business entities. In the past, they had to manually add any new service centers in each sandbox in the code migration path, they would like to eliminate the manual work if it is possible.What is an optimal way to accomplish this requirement?
column4: A. Add the service centers to a list custom settings. | B. Define a brand-new custom object with a picklist field to host all of the service centers. | C. Add all of the service centers to a custom metadata type. | D. Add the service centers to a hierarchical custom settings.
column6: Custom metadata types (C) are the optimal solution for reference data that needs to automatically deploy with metadata deployments across environments — they are packaged with code, require no manual data loading per environment, and are queryable in Apex/formula fields. List custom settings (A) and hierarchical custom settings (D) require manual data entry per environment. A custom object (B) also requires separate data loading.

column3: What are two roles a project Steering Committee plays in determining what methodologies are used? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Designing a methodology that will meet a particular project's requirements | B. Enforcing that corporate project stage gates are part of the chosen methodology | C. Approving deviations from the chosen methodology, when required to address project issues | D. Setting the criteria for selecting Agile or Waterfall methodology to be used on internal projects
column6: Steering Committee roles related to methodology: enforcing that corporate project stage gates are included in the chosen methodology (B) — ensuring governance compliance; and setting the criteria for selecting Agile or Waterfall for internal projects (D) — defining the decision framework. Designing specific methodology details (A) is the project manager's or agile coach's responsibility. Approving methodology deviations (C) is typically a Change Control Board function.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is launching a new sneaker line during the upcoming holiday season and needs to do a thorough batch data testing before Go-Live. CK is using Salesforce unlimited edition.What two sandbox types should the architect recommend for batch data testing?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Full sandbox | B. Partial Copy sandbox | C. Developer Pro sandbox | D. Developer sandbox
column6: For batch data testing requiring thorough volume testing in Salesforce Unlimited Edition: Full sandbox (A) provides a complete copy of production data for maximum realism; Partial Copy sandbox (B) provides up to 10,000 records per object which may be sufficient for batch testing scenarios. Developer Pro (C) and Developer (D) sandboxes have very limited storage unsuitable for large-volume batch data testing.

column3: What would a technical architect recommend to avoid possible delays while deploying a change set?
column4: A. Manually create new custom objects and new custom fields. | B. Change set performance is independent of included components. | C. Manually apply the field type changes. | D. Manually validate change sets before deployment.
column6: Manually validating (running a Check Only deployment) the change set before actual deployment (D) allows the system to identify issues — missing dependencies, test failures, component conflicts — in advance, reducing deployment delays. Manual creation of objects/fields (A,C) handles components not in the change set. Change set performance is NOT independent of components (B is false — profile changes in particular increase deployment time significantly).
